revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.text: #redirect[[en:Kōzō_Nihonmatsu]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''二本松 孝蔵'''（にほんまつ こうぞう、[[1884年]]（[[明治]]17年）3月26日<ref name=jinmei>『大衆人事録 昭和15年改訂13版』570頁。</ref> - [[1972年]]（[[昭和]]47年9月9日）は、日本の建築家、文化財修理技師。多数の社寺の保存修理や新築設計を手掛けた。
== 経歴等 == 福島県出身<ref name=jinmei/>。[[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）工手学校（現[[工学院大学]]）卒業<ref name=jinmei/>。[[1911年]]（[[明治]]44年）、国宝建造物修理工事主任。延命寺地蔵堂修理などを手掛ける。[[1917年]]（[[大正]]6年）に造神宮技手兼明治神宮造営技手となったのち、[[1927]]]年（[[昭和]]2年）国粋建築研究所を開設<ref name=kensetu>『建設界紳士録 関東編』1962年、769頁。</ref>。[[1925年]]（[[大正}}14年）大正大震災記念建造物競技設計佳作。[[角南隆]]を中心とした[[神祇院造営課]]と連動し、神社では11府県社の護国神社や官国幣社の社務所、齋館などの設計のほか、寺院でも比叡山、身延山祖廟設営など社寺ともに多くの設計を手掛けた<ref name=kouron>『宗教公論』第27巻10号、宗教問題研究所1957年、10頁。</ref>。
戦後は一時故郷でカラカサを作ろうかとも考えたというが<ref>「時の人＝二本松孝蔵」（『毎日新聞』1960.10.29 東京朝刊）</ref>、[[1946年]]（[[昭和]]21年）比叡山延暦寺修理技師拝命につづき、[[1948年]]（[[昭和]]23年）から日光東照宮営繕課技師を拝命し、日光国宝保存工事事務所技師として昭和の大修理に尽力した。事務所は社寺建築研究所と改めている<ref>『日本叙勲者名鑑 昭和39年4月〜昭和53年4月 上』、1978年、1414頁</ref>。
[[1958]]年（昭和33年）、栃木県文化功労章、[[1966年]]（昭和41年）4月勲四等[[瑞宝章]]。 == 作品 == ===設計=== * 小高区天野家住宅（[[1913年]]）<ref>速水清孝・狩野勝重「建築技師・二本松孝蔵の経歴と「天野家住宅」について」（『日本建築学会東北支部研究報告集. 計画系』2016-06）</ref> * [[山内神社]]社殿（[[1932年]]）※空襲焼失。<ref>『別格官幣社山内神社御造営誌』山内豊信公・山内豊範公別格官幣社奉祀期成会、1935。</ref> * [[鹿児島神宮]]社殿（[[?年]]）<ref name=shino/> * [[出雲大社]]勅使館、齋館、社務所（[[1933年]]）。<ref>二本松孝蔵「出雲大社勅使館齋館社務所の建築」（『東洋建築』1巻2号、相模書房、1937）</ref> * [[小戸神社]]社殿（[[1934年]]）<ref>二本松孝蔵「小戶神社の改築計畫」（『東洋建築』1巻1号、相模書房、1937）</ref>。 * [[埼玉県護国神社]]社殿（[[1934年]]）<ref>『埼玉県史』第七巻、埼玉県、1939年</ref> * [[名和神社]]社殿（[[1935年]]）<ref>二本松孝蔵「名和神社拝殿」（『東洋建築』1巻4号、相模書房、1937）</ref> * [[枚聞神社]]社殿（[[1936年]]）<ref name=shino/> * [[福島県護国神社]]社殿（[[1937年]]）<ref>二本松孝蔵「福島縣官祭信夫山招魂社」（『東洋建築』1巻1号、相模書房、1937）</ref> * [[宮崎神宮]]徴古館（[[1940年]]）※現存せず<ref>『紀元二千六百年祝典記録』第七冊、1940年</ref> * [[栃木県護国神社]]社殿（[[1940年]]） * [[大宮氷川神社]]社務所、勅使齋館（[[1940年]]）<ref name=shino>篠原直通・渡辺 洋子「大宮氷川神社に関する研究 角南隆と国粋建築研究所」（『建築歴史・意匠』 (2016) 359-360 日本建築学会、2016年 ）</ref> * [[秋田県護国神社]]社殿（[[1940年]]）※現存せず<ref name=shino/> * [[豊玉姫神社 (南九州市)]]社殿（[[1940年]]）<ref>『知覧郷土史』知覧町、1962年</ref> * [[伊奈波神社]]社殿（[[1932年]]-[[1940年]]）<ref>『伊奈波神社略誌』伊奈波神社、1941年</ref> * [[宮城県護国神社]]（[[1942年]]）※空襲焼失<ref>『宮城県史』13（美術・建築）宮城県史刊行会、1980年</ref> * [[湊川神社]]神門（[[1943年]]）<ref>神山極三「湊川神社の神門」（『旅と伝説』16巻7号、1943年）</ref> * [[鹿児島県護国神社]]社殿（[[1948年]]）<ref name=shino/> * [[大洗磯前神社]]一の鳥居（内藤多仲共）（[[1963年]]）<ref>調査実績:福島県内に残る旧修験者活動遺構調査に関する覚書（3）（http://www.anno-spero.org/renaissance/dentou/history/fukushima_3.html）</ref> == 著作等 == * 「日本建築技法講座」（1）〜（3）（檜皮葺の研究、萱葺の研究、柿葺の硏究） （『東洋建築』1巻8号-2巻1号、相模書房、1937-38年） * 「塔」（『大日光』9号、日光東照宮、1957年） * 「日光社寺殿堂修理に就而」（『古建築』）
== 出典 == <references /> {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 「二本松孝蔵翁文化功労章を受く」（『宗教公論』第27巻10号、宗教問題研究所、1957年、10頁）。 * 篠原直通・渡辺 洋子「大宮氷川神社に関する研究 角南隆と国粋建築研究所」（『建築歴史・意匠』 (2016) 359-360 日本建築学会、2016年
{{Normdaten}}

[[1958]]年（昭和33年）、栃木県文化功労章、[[1966年]]（昭和41年）4月勲四等[[瑞宝章]]。 == 作品 == ===設計=== * 小高区天野家住宅（[[1913年]]）<ref>速水清孝・狩野勝重「建築技師・二本松孝蔵の経歴と「天野家住宅」について」（『日本建築学会東北支部研究報告集. 計画系』2016-06）</ref> * [[山内神社]]社殿（[[1932年]]）※空襲焼失。<ref>『別格官幣社山内神社御造営誌』山内豊信公・山内豊範公別格官幣社奉祀期成会、1935。</ref> * [[鹿児島神宮]]社殿（[[?年]]）<ref name=shino/> * [[出雲大社]]勅使館、齋館、社務所（[[1933年]]）。<ref>二本松孝蔵「出雲大社勅使館齋館社務所の建築」（『東洋建築』1巻2号、相模書房、1937）</ref> * [[小戸神社]]社殿（[[1934年]]）<ref>二本松孝蔵「小戶神社の改築計畫」（『東洋建築』1巻1号、相模書房、1937）</ref>。 * [[埼玉県護国神社]]社殿（[[1934年]]）<ref>『埼玉県史』第七巻、埼玉県、1939年</ref> * [[名和神社]]社殿（[[1935年]]）<ref>二本松孝蔵「名和神社拝殿」（『東洋建築』1巻4号、相模書房、1937）</ref> * [[枚聞神社]]社殿（[[1936年]]）<ref name=shino/> * [[福島県護国神社]]社殿（[[1937年]]）<ref>二本松孝蔵「福島縣官祭信夫山招魂社」（『東洋建築』1巻1号、相模書房、1937）</ref> * [[宮崎神宮]]徴古館（[[1940年]]）※現存せず<ref>『紀元二千六百年祝典記録』第七冊、1940年</ref> * [[栃木県護国神社]]社殿（[[1940年]]） * [[大宮氷川神社]]社務所、勅使齋館（[[1940年]]）<ref name=shino>篠原直通・渡辺 洋子「大宮氷川神社に関する研究 角南隆と国粋建築研究所」（『建築歴史・意匠』 (2016) 359-360 日本建築学会、2016年 ）</ref> * [[秋田県護国神社]]社殿（[[1940年]]）※現存せず<ref name=shino/> * [[豊玉姫神社 (南九州市)]]社殿（[[1940年]]）<ref>『知覧郷土史』知覧町、1962年</ref> * [[伊奈波神社]]社殿（[[1932年]]-[[1940年]]）<ref>『伊奈波神社略誌』伊奈波神社、1941年</ref> * [[宮城県護国神社]]（[[1942年]]）※空襲焼失<ref>『宮城県史』13（美術・建築）宮城県史刊行会、1980年</ref> * [[湊川神社]]神門（[[1943年]]）<ref>神山極三「湊川神社の神門」（『旅と伝説』16巻7号、1943年）</ref> * [[鹿児島県護国神社]]社殿（[[1948年]]）<ref name=shino/> * [[大洗磯前神社]]一の鳥居（内藤多仲共）（[[1963年]]）<ref>調査実績:福島県内に残る旧修験者活動遺構調査に関する覚書（3）（http://www.anno-spero.org/renaissance/dentou/history/fukushima_3.html）</ref> == 著作等 == * 「日本建築技法講座」（1）〜（3）（檜皮葺の研究、萱葺の研究、柿葺の硏究） （『東洋建築』1巻8号-2巻1号、相模書房、1937-38年） * 「塔」（『大日光』9号、日光東照宮、1957年） * 「日光社寺殿堂修理に就而」（『古建築』）

'''二本松 孝蔵'''（にほんまつ こうぞう、[[1884年]]（[[明治]]17年）3月26日『大衆人事録 昭和15年改訂13版』570頁。 - [[1972年]]（[[昭和]]47年9月9日）は、日本の建築家、文化財修理技師。多数の社寺の保存修理や新築設計を手掛けた。 {{Infobox 建築家 |image = File:二本松孝蔵.png |image_size = 200px |caption = 『別格官幣社山内神社御造営誌』より |name = にほんまつ こうぞう
二本松 孝蔵 |nationality = {{JPN}} |birth_date = [[1884年]][[3月]][[26日]] |birth_place = [[福島県]]相馬郡小高町岡田 |death_date = {{死亡年月日と没年齢|1884|3|26|1972|9|9}} |death_place = 東京都 |alma_mater = 工手学校（現[[工学院大学]]） |practice_name = [[内務省]]
国粋建築研究所
[[日光東照宮]] |significant_buildings= [[伊奈波神社]]
など |significant_projects = |significant_design = |literary_works = |awards = }} == 経歴等 == 福島県出身。[[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）工手学校（現[[工学院大学]]）卒業。[[1911年]]（[[明治]]44年）、国宝建造物修理工事主任。延命寺地蔵堂修理などを手掛ける。[[1917年]]（[[大正]]6年）に造神宮技手兼明治神宮造営技手となったのち、[[1927]]]年（[[昭和]]2年）国粋建築研究所を開設『建設界紳士録 関東編』1962年、769頁。。[[1925年]]（[[大正]]14年）大正大震災記念建造物競技設計佳作。[[角南隆]]を中心とした[[神祇院造営課]]と連動し、神社では11府県社の護国神社や官国幣社の社務所、齋館などの設計のほか、寺院でも比叡山、身延山祖廟設営など社寺ともに多くの設計を手掛けた『宗教公論』第27巻10号、宗教問題研究所1957年、10頁。。 戦後は一時故郷でカラカサを作ろうかとも考えたというが「時の人＝二本松孝蔵」（『毎日新聞』1960.10.29 東京朝刊）、[[1946年]]（[[昭和]]21年）比叡山延暦寺修理技師拝命につづき、[[1948年]]（[[昭和]]23年）から日光東照宮営繕課技師を拝命し、日光国宝保存工事事務所技師として昭和の大修理に尽力した。事務所は社寺建築研究所と改めている『日本叙勲者名鑑 昭和39年4月〜昭和53年4月 上』、1978年、1414頁。 [[1958]]年（昭和33年）、栃木県文化功労章、[[1966年]]（昭和41年）4月勲四等[[瑞宝章]]。 == 作品 == ===設計=== [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|名和神社]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|豊玉姫神社]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|伊奈波神社]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|湊川神社神門]] * 小高区天野家住宅（[[1913年]]）速水清孝・狩野勝重「建築技師・二本松孝蔵の経歴と「天野家住宅」について」（『日本建築学会東北支部研究報告集. 計画系』2016-06） * [[山内神社]]社殿（[[1932年]]）※空襲焼失。『別格官幣社山内神社御造営誌』山内豊信公・山内豊範公別格官幣社奉祀期成会、1935。 * [[鹿児島神宮]]社殿（[[?年]]） * [[出雲大社]]勅使館、齋館、社務所（[[1933年]]）。二本松孝蔵「出雲大社勅使館齋館社務所の建築」（『東洋建築』1巻2号、相模書房、1937） * [[小戸神社]]社殿（[[1934年]]）二本松孝蔵「小戶神社の改築計畫」（『東洋建築』1巻1号、相模書房、1937）。 * [[埼玉県護国神社]]社殿（[[1934年]]）『埼玉県史』第七巻、埼玉県、1939年 * [[名和神社]]社殿（[[1935年]]）二本松孝蔵「名和神社拝殿」（『東洋建築』1巻4号、相模書房、1937） * [[枚聞神社]]社殿（[[1936年]]） * [[福島県護国神社]]社殿（[[1937年]]）二本松孝蔵「福島縣官祭信夫山招魂社」（『東洋建築』1巻1号、相模書房、1937） * [[福島稲荷神社]]社殿（[[1937年]]）『福島市史』第5巻、福島市教育委員会、1975年、790頁 * [[宮崎神宮]]徴古館（[[1940年]]）※現存せず『紀元二千六百年祝典記録』第七冊、1940年 * [[栃木県護国神社]]社殿（[[1940年]]） * [[大宮氷川神社]]社務所、勅使齋館（[[1940年]]）篠原直通・渡辺 洋子「大宮氷川神社に関する研究 角南隆と国粋建築研究所」（『建築歴史・意匠』 (2016) 359-360 日本建築学会、2016年 ） * [[秋田県護国神社]]社殿（[[1940年]]）※現存せず * [[豊玉姫神社 (南九州市)]]社殿（[[1940年]]）『知覧郷土史』知覧町、1962年 * [[伊奈波神社]]社殿（[[1932年]]-[[1940年]]）『伊奈波神社略誌』伊奈波神社、1941年 * [[宮城県護国神社]]（[[1942年]]）※空襲焼失『宮城県史』13（美術・建築）宮城県史刊行会、1980年 * [[湊川神社]]神門（[[1943年]]）神山極三「湊川神社の神門」（『旅と伝説』16巻7号、1943年） * [[鹿児島県護国神社]]社殿（[[1948年]]） * [[総持寺]]大祖堂（内藤多仲共）（[[1962年]]） * [[大洗磯前神社]]一の鳥居（内藤多仲共）（[[1963年]]）調査実績:福島県内に残る旧修験者活動遺構調査に関する覚書（3）（http://www.anno-spero.org/renaissance/dentou/history/fukushima_3.html） == 著作等 == * 「日本建築技法講座」（1）〜（3）（檜皮葺の研究、萱葺の研究、柿葺の硏究） （『東洋建築』1巻8号-2巻1号、相模書房、1937-38年） * 「塔」（『大日光』9号、日光東照宮、1957年） * 「日光社寺殿堂修理に就而」（『古建築』） == 出典 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 「二本松孝蔵翁文化功労章を受く」（『宗教公論』第27巻10号、宗教問題研究所、1957年、10頁）。 * 篠原直通・渡辺 洋子「大宮氷川神社に関する研究 角南隆と国粋建築研究所」（『建築歴史・意匠』 (2016) 359-360 日本建築学会、2016年 == 関連項目 == * [[日光東照宮]] * [[神祇院造営課]] * [[工学院大学]] * [[角南隆]] * [[護国神社]] {{Normdaten}} {{デフォルトソート:にほんまつ こうぞう}} [[Category:20世紀日本の建築家]] [[Category:日本の内務省関係者|築]] [[Category:工学院大学出身の人物]] [[Category:福島県出身の人物]] [[Category:1884年生]] [[Category:1972年没]]

など |significant_projects = |significant_design = |literary_works = |awards = }} '''二本松 孝蔵'''（にほんまつ こうぞう、[[1884年]]（[[明治]]17年）[[3月26日]]『大衆人事録 昭和15年改訂13版』570頁。 - [[1972年]]（[[昭和]]47年）[[9月9日]]）は、日本の[[建築家]]、[[文化財]]修理技師。多数の社寺の保存修理や新築[[設計]]を手掛けた。 == 経歴等 == 福島県出身。[[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）工手学校（現[[工学院大学]]）卒業。[[1911年]]（[[明治]]44年）、[[国宝]]建造物修理工事主任。[[延命寺]]地蔵堂修理などを手掛ける。[[1917年]]（[[大正]]6年）に造神宮技手兼[[明治神宮]]造営技手となったのち、[[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）国粋建築研究所を開設『建設界紳士録 関東編』1962年、769頁。。[[1925年]]（[[大正]]14年）大正大震災記念建造物競技設計佳作。[[角南隆]]を中心とした[[神祇院造営課]]と連動し、[[神社]]では11府県社の[[護国神社]]や[[官国幣社]]の社務所、齋館などの設計のほか、[[寺院]]でも[[比叡山]]修理、[[身延山]]祖廟設営など社寺ともに多くの設計を手掛けた『宗教公論』第27巻10号、宗教問題研究所1957年、10頁。。 戦後は一時故郷でカラカサを作ろうかとも考えたというが「時の人＝二本松孝蔵」（『毎日新聞』1960.10.29 東京朝刊）、[[1946年]]（[[昭和]]21年）[[比叡山延暦寺]]修理技師拝命につづき、[[1948年]]（[[昭和]]23年）から[[日光東照宮]][[営繕]]課技師を拝命し、日光国宝保存工事事務所技師として昭和の大修理に尽力した。事務所は社寺建築研究所と改めている『日本叙勲者名鑑 昭和39年4月〜昭和53年4月 上』、1978年、1414頁。 [[1958年]]（昭和33年）、[[栃木県]]文化功労章、[[1966年]]（昭和41年）4月勲四等[[瑞宝章]]。 == 作品 == ===設計=== [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|名和神社]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|豊玉姫神社]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|伊奈波神社]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|湊川神社神門]] * 小高区天野家住宅（[[1913年]]）速水清孝・狩野勝重「建築技師・二本松孝蔵の経歴と「天野家住宅」について」（『日本建築学会東北支部研究報告集. 計画系』2016-06） * [[山内神社]]社殿（[[1932年]]）※空襲焼失。『別格官幣社山内神社御造営誌』山内豊信公・山内豊範公別格官幣社奉祀期成会、1935。 * [[鹿児島神宮]]社殿（[[?年]]） * [[出雲大社]]勅使館、齋館、社務所（[[1933年]]）。二本松孝蔵「出雲大社勅使館齋館社務所の建築」（『東洋建築』1巻2号、相模書房、1937） * [[小戸神社]]社殿（[[1934年]]）二本松孝蔵「小戶神社の改築計畫」（『東洋建築』1巻1号、相模書房、1937）。 * [[埼玉県護国神社]]社殿（[[1934年]]）『埼玉県史』第七巻、埼玉県、1939年 * [[名和神社]]社殿（[[1935年]]）二本松孝蔵「名和神社拝殿」（『東洋建築』1巻4号、相模書房、1937） * [[枚聞神社]]社殿（[[1936年]]） * [[福島県護国神社]]社殿（[[1937年]]）二本松孝蔵「福島縣官祭信夫山招魂社」（『東洋建築』1巻1号、相模書房、1937） * [[福島稲荷神社]]社殿（[[1937年]]）『福島市史』第5巻、福島市教育委員会、1975年、790頁 * [[宮崎神宮]]徴古館（[[1940年]]）※現存せず『紀元二千六百年祝典記録』第七冊、1940年 * [[栃木県護国神社]]社殿（[[1940年]]） * [[大宮氷川神社]]社務所、勅使齋館（[[1940年]]）篠原直通・渡辺 洋子「大宮氷川神社に関する研究 角南隆と国粋建築研究所」（『建築歴史・意匠』 (2016) 359-360 日本建築学会、2016年 ） * [[秋田県護国神社]]社殿（[[1940年]]）※現存せず * [[豊玉姫神社 (南九州市)]]社殿（[[1940年]]）『知覧郷土史』知覧町、1962年 * [[伊奈波神社]]社殿（[[1932年]]-[[1940年]]）『伊奈波神社略誌』伊奈波神社、1941年 * [[宮城県護国神社]]（[[1942年]]）※空襲焼失『宮城県史』13（美術・建築）宮城県史刊行会、1980年 * [[湊川神社]]神門（[[1943年]]）神山極三「湊川神社の神門」（『旅と伝説』16巻7号、1943年） * [[鹿児島県護国神社]]社殿（[[1948年]]） * [[総持寺]]大祖堂（内藤多仲共）（[[1962年]]） * [[大洗磯前神社]]一の鳥居（内藤多仲共）（[[1963年]]）調査実績:福島県内に残る旧修験者活動遺構調査に関する覚書（3）（http://www.anno-spero.org/renaissance/dentou/history/fukushima_3.html） == 著作等 == * 「日本建築技法講座」（1）〜（3）（檜皮葺の研究、萱葺の研究、柿葺の硏究） （『東洋建築』1巻8号-2巻1号、相模書房、1937-38年） * 「塔」（『大日光』9号、日光東照宮、1957年） * 「日光社寺殿堂修理に就而」（『古建築』） == 出典 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 「二本松孝蔵翁文化功労章を受く」（『宗教公論』第27巻10号、宗教問題研究所、1957年、10頁）。 * 篠原直通・渡辺 洋子「大宮氷川神社に関する研究 角南隆と国粋建築研究所」（『建築歴史・意匠』 (2016) 359-360 日本建築学会、2016年 == 関連項目 == * [[日光東照宮]] * [[神祇院造営課]] * [[工学院大学]] * [[角南隆]] * [[護国神社]] {{Normdaten}} {{デフォルトソート:にほんまつ こうぞう}} [[Category:20世紀日本の建築家]] [[Category:日本の内務省関係者|築]] [[Category:工学院大学出身の人物]] [[Category:福島県出身の人物]] [[Category:1884年生]] [[Category:1972年没]]

[[日光東照宮]] |significant_buildings= [[伊奈波神社]]
など |significant_projects = |significant_design = |literary_works = |awards = }} '''二本松 孝蔵'''（にほんまつ こうぞう、[[1884年]]（[[明治]]17年）[[3月26日]]『大衆人事録 昭和15年改訂13版』570頁。 - [[1972年]]（[[昭和]]47年）[[9月9日]]）は、日本の[[建築家]]、[[文化財]]修理技師。多数の社寺の保存修理や新築[[設計]]を手掛けた。 == 経歴等 == 福島県出身。[[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）工手学校（現[[工学院大学]]）卒業。[[1911年]]（[[明治]]44年）、[[国宝]]建造物修理工事主任。[[延命寺]]地蔵堂修理などを手掛ける。[[1917年]]（[[大正]]6年）に造神宮技手兼[[明治神宮]]造営技手となったのち、[[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）国粋建築研究所を開設『建設界紳士録 関東編』1962年、769頁。。[[1925年]]（[[大正]]14年）大正大震災記念建造物競技設計佳作。[[角南隆]]を中心とした[[神祇院造営課]]と連動し、[[神社]]では11府県社の[[護国神社]]や[[官国幣社]]の社務所、齋館などの設計のほか、[[寺院]]でも[[比叡山]]修理、[[身延山]]祖廟設営など社寺ともに多くの設計を手掛けた『宗教公論』第27巻10号、宗教問題研究所1957年、10頁。。 戦後は一時故郷でカラカサを作ろうかとも考えたというが「時の人＝二本松孝蔵」（『毎日新聞』1960.10.29 東京朝刊）、[[1946年]]（[[昭和]]21年）[[比叡山延暦寺]]修理技師拝命につづき、[[1948年]]（[[昭和]]23年）から[[日光東照宮]][[営繕]]課技師を拝命し、日光国宝保存工事事務所技師として昭和の大修理に尽力した。事務所は社寺建築研究所と改めている『日本叙勲者名鑑 昭和39年4月〜昭和53年4月 上』、1978年、1414頁。 [[1958年]]（昭和33年）、[[栃木県]]文化功労章、[[1966年]]（昭和41年）4月勲四等[[瑞宝章]]。 == 作品 == ===設計=== [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|名和神社]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|豊玉姫神社]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|伊奈波神社]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|湊川神社神門]] * 小高区天野家住宅（[[1913年]]）速水清孝・狩野勝重「建築技師・二本松孝蔵の経歴と「天野家住宅」について」（『日本建築学会東北支部研究報告集. 計画系』2016-06） * [[山内神社]]社殿（[[1932年]]）※空襲焼失。『別格官幣社山内神社御造営誌』山内豊信公・山内豊範公別格官幣社奉祀期成会、1935。 * [[鹿児島神宮]]社殿（[[?年]]） * [[出雲大社]]勅使館、齋館、社務所（[[1933年]]）。二本松孝蔵「出雲大社勅使館齋館社務所の建築」（『東洋建築』1巻2号、相模書房、1937） * [[小戸神社]]社殿（[[1934年]]）二本松孝蔵「小戶神社の改築計畫」（『東洋建築』1巻1号、相模書房、1937）。 * [[埼玉県護国神社]]社殿（[[1934年]]）『埼玉県史』第七巻、埼玉県、1939年 * [[名和神社]]社殿（[[1935年]]）二本松孝蔵「名和神社拝殿」（『東洋建築』1巻4号、相模書房、1937） * [[枚聞神社]]社殿（[[1936年]]） * [[福島県護国神社]]社殿（[[1937年]]）二本松孝蔵「福島縣官祭信夫山招魂社」（『東洋建築』1巻1号、相模書房、1937） * [[福島稲荷神社]]社殿（[[1937年]]）『福島市史』第5巻、福島市教育委員会、1975年、790頁 * [[宮崎神宮]]徴古館（[[1940年]]）※現存せず『紀元二千六百年祝典記録』第七冊、1940年 * [[栃木県護国神社]]社殿（[[1940年]]） * [[大宮氷川神社]]社務所、勅使齋館（[[1940年]]）篠原直通・渡辺 洋子「大宮氷川神社に関する研究 角南隆と国粋建築研究所」（『建築歴史・意匠』 (2016) 359-360 日本建築学会、2016年 ） * [[秋田県護国神社]]社殿（[[1940年]]）※現存せず * [[豊玉姫神社 (南九州市)]]社殿（[[1940年]]）『知覧郷土史』知覧町、1962年 * [[伊奈波神社]]社殿（[[1932年]]-[[1940年]]）『伊奈波神社略誌』伊奈波神社、1941年 * [[宮城県護国神社]]（[[1942年]]）※空襲焼失『宮城県史』13（美術・建築）宮城県史刊行会、1980年 * [[湊川神社]]神門（[[1943年]]）神山極三「湊川神社の神門」（『旅と伝説』16巻7号、1943年） * [[鹿児島県護国神社]]社殿（[[1948年]]） * [[總持寺]]大祖堂（内藤多仲共）（[[1962年]]） * [[大洗磯前神社]]一の鳥居（内藤多仲共）（[[1963年]]）調査実績:福島県内に残る旧修験者活動遺構調査に関する覚書（3）（http://www.anno-spero.org/renaissance/dentou/history/fukushima_3.html） == 著作等 == * 「日本建築技法講座」（1）〜（3）（檜皮葺の研究、萱葺の研究、柿葺の硏究） （『東洋建築』1巻8号-2巻1号、相模書房、1937-38年） * 「塔」（『大日光』9号、日光東照宮、1957年） * 「日光社寺殿堂修理に就而」（『古建築』） == 出典 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 「二本松孝蔵翁文化功労章を受く」（『宗教公論』第27巻10号、宗教問題研究所、1957年、10頁）。 * 篠原直通・渡辺 洋子「大宮氷川神社に関する研究 角南隆と国粋建築研究所」（『建築歴史・意匠』 (2016) 359-360 日本建築学会、2016年 == 関連項目 == * [[日光東照宮]] * [[神祇院造営課]] * [[工学院大学]] * [[角南隆]] * [[護国神社]] {{Normdaten}} {{デフォルトソート:にほんまつ こうぞう}} [[Category:20世紀日本の建築家]] [[Category:内務省 (日本)|築]] [[Category:工学院大学出身の人物]] [[Category:福島県出身の人物]] [[Category:1884年生]] [[Category:1972年没]]

{{Infobox Architect | image = File:二本松孝蔵.png | image_size = 200px | caption = From "別格官幣社山内神社御造営誌" | name = Nihonmatsu Kōzō
二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = [[March 26]], [[1884]] | birth_place = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], Soma District, Odaka Town, Okada | death_date = {{Death date and age|1884|3|26|1972|9|9}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = Kote School (now [[Kogakuin University]]) | practice_name = [[Home Ministry (Japan)|Home Ministry]]
Kokui Architecture Research Institute
[[Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] | significant_buildings= [[Inaba Shrine]]
others | significant_projects = | significant_design = | literary_works = | awards = }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, March 26, 1884『大衆人事録 昭和15年改訂13版』570頁。 – September 9, 1972) was a Japanese [[architect]] and cultural property repair technician. He was involved in numerous preservation and repair projects and designed many new shrine and temple buildings. == Career == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. He graduated from Kote School (now [[Kogakuin University]]) in 1908. In 1911, he became the chief for repairs to [[National Treasures of Japan|national treasure]] buildings, including the repair of the Jizō Hall at [[Enmeiji]]. In 1917, he became a technical officer at the Imperial Shrine and Meiji Shrine. In 1927, he established the Kokui Architecture Research Institute『建設界紳士録 関東編』1962年、769頁。. His work included projects such as the construction of commemorative buildings for the Great Taisho Earthquake and designs for shrines and temples in coordination with the {{ill|Jingiin Construction Division|ja|神祇院造営課}}, including various prefectural shrines and [[Gokoku shrines]]『宗教公論』第27巻10号、宗教問題研究所1957年、10頁。. After the war, Nihonmatsu considered starting a business making umbrellas in his hometown「時の人＝二本松孝蔵」（『毎日新聞』1960.10.29 東京朝刊） but resumed his architectural career. In 1946, he was appointed a repair technician for [[Mount Hiei]]'s [[Enryakuji]]. From 1948, he worked on large-scale preservation efforts at [[Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] as a technician in its maintenance department. His office was renamed the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute『日本叙勲者名鑑 昭和39年4月〜昭和53年4月 上』、1978年、1414頁。. In 1958, Nihonmatsu was awarded the Tochigi Prefecture Cultural Merit Award, and in 1966, he received the Fourth Class Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Works == === Designs === [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka District (1913)速水清孝・狩野勝重「建築技師・二本松孝蔵の経歴と「天野家住宅」について」（『日本建築学会東北支部研究報告集. 計画系』2016-06） * [[Yamauchi Shrine]] buildings (1932; destroyed during air raids)『別格官幣社山内神社御造営誌』山内豊信公・山内豊範公別格官幣社奉祀期成会、1935。 * [[Izumo Taisha]] envoy hall, ritual hall, and office (1933)二本松孝蔵「出雲大社勅使館齋館社務所の建築」（『東洋建築』1巻2号、相模書房、1937） * [[Saitama Gokoku Shrine]] buildings (1934)『埼玉県史』第七巻、埼玉県、1939年 * [[Nawa Shrine]] buildings (1935)二本松孝蔵「名和神社拝殿」（『東洋建築』1巻4号、相模書房、1937） * [[Inaba Shrine]] buildings (1932–1940)『伊奈波神社略誌』伊奈波神社、1941年 * [[Minatogawa Shrine]] gate (1943)神山極三「湊川神社の神門」（『旅と伝説』16巻7号、1943年） == Publications == * "Japanese Architectural Techniques" series (Research on Hinoki Bark Roofing, Thatched Roofing, and Wooden Shingle Roofing), 1937–38 * "The Tower," {{ill|Dainikkō|ja|大日光}}, 1957 * "Repair of Shrines and Temples at Nikko," in {{ill|Ancient Architecture|ja|古建築}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] * {{ill|Jingiin Construction Division|ja|神祇院造営課}} * [[Kogakuin University]] * [[Sumiyoshi Ryō]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] [[Category:20th-century Japanese architects]] [[Category:Home Ministry officials]] [[Category:Kogakuin University alumni]] [[Category:People from Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:1884 births]] [[Category:1972 deaths]] [[ja:二本松孝蔵]]

His office was renamed the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute『日本叙勲者名鑑 昭和39年4月〜昭和53年4月 上』、1978年、1414頁。. In 1958, Nihonmatsu was awarded the Tochigi Prefecture Cultural Merit Award, and in 1966, he received the Fourth Class Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Works == === Designs === [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka District (1913)速水清孝・狩野勝重「建築技師・二本松孝蔵の経歴と「天野家住宅」について」（『日本建築学会東北支部研究報告集. 計画系』2016-06） * [[Yamauchi Shrine]] buildings (1932; destroyed during air raids)『別格官幣社山内神社御造営誌』山内豊信公・山内豊範公別格官幣社奉祀期成会、1935。 * [[Izumo Taisha]] envoy hall, ritual hall, and office (1933)二本松孝蔵「出雲大社勅使館齋館社務所の建築」（『東洋建築』1巻2号、相模書房、1937） * [[Saitama Gokoku Shrine]] buildings (1934)『埼玉県史』第七巻、埼玉県、1939年 * [[Nawa Shrine]] buildings (1935)二本松孝蔵「名和神社拝殿」（『東洋建築』1巻4号、相模書房、1937） * [[Inaba Shrine]] buildings (1932–1940)『伊奈波神社略誌』伊奈波神社、1941年 * [[Minatogawa Shrine]] gate (1943)神山極三「湊川神社の神門」（『旅と伝説』16巻7号、1943年） == Publications == * "Japanese Architectural Techniques" series (Research on Hinoki Bark Roofing, Thatched Roofing, and Wooden Shingle Roofing), 1937–38 * "The Tower," {{ill|Dainikkō|ja|大日光}}, 1957 * "Repair of Shrines and Temples at Nikko," in {{ill|Ancient Architecture|ja|古建築}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] * {{ill|Jingiin Construction Division|ja|神祇院造営課}} * [[Kogakuin University]] * [[Sumiyoshi Ryō]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] [[Category:20th-century Japanese architects]] [[Category:Home Ministry officials]] [[Category:Kogakuin University alumni]] [[Category:People from Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:1884 births]] [[Category:1972 deaths]] [[Category:original pages]] [[ja:二本松孝蔵]]

[[Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] | significant_buildings= [[Inaba Shrine]]
others | significant_projects = | significant_design = | literary_works = | awards = }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, March 26, 1884『大衆人事録 昭和15年改訂13版』570頁。 – September 9, 1972) was a Japanese [[architect]] and cultural property repair technician. He was involved in numerous preservation and repair projects and designed many new shrine and temple buildings. == Career == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. He graduated from Kote School (now [[Kogakuin University]]) in 1908. In 1911, he became the chief for repairs to [[National Treasures of Japan|national treasure]] buildings, including the repair of the Jizō Hall at [[Enmeiji]]. In 1917, he became a technical officer at the Imperial Shrine and Meiji Shrine. In 1927, he established the Kokui Architecture Research Institute『建設界紳士録 関東編』1962年、769頁。. His work included projects such as the construction of commemorative buildings for the Great Taisho Earthquake and designs for shrines and temples in coordination with the {{ill|Jingiin Construction Division|ja|神祇院造営課}}, including various prefectural shrines and [[Gokoku shrines]]『宗教公論』第27巻10号、宗教問題研究所1957年、10頁。. After the war, Nihonmatsu considered starting a business making umbrellas in his hometown「時の人＝二本松孝蔵」（『毎日新聞』1960.10.29 東京朝刊） but resumed his architectural career. In 1946, he was appointed a repair technician for [[Mount Hiei]]'s [[Enryakuji]]. From 1948, he worked on large-scale preservation efforts at [[Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] as a technician in its maintenance department. His office was renamed the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute『日本叙勲者名鑑 昭和39年4月〜昭和53年4月 上』、1978年、1414頁。. In 1958, Nihonmatsu was awarded the Tochigi Prefecture Cultural Merit Award, and in 1966, he received the Fourth Class Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Works == === Designs === [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka District (1913)速水清孝・狩野勝重「建築技師・二本松孝蔵の経歴と「天野家住宅」について」（『日本建築学会東北支部研究報告集. 計画系』2016-06） * [[Yamauchi Shrine]] buildings (1932; destroyed during air raids)『別格官幣社山内神社御造営誌』山内豊信公・山内豊範公別格官幣社奉祀期成会、1935。 * [[Izumo Taisha]] envoy hall, ritual hall, and office (1933)二本松孝蔵「出雲大社勅使館齋館社務所の建築」（『東洋建築』1巻2号、相模書房、1937） * [[Saitama Gokoku Shrine]] buildings (1934)『埼玉県史』第七巻、埼玉県、1939年 * [[Nawa Shrine]] buildings (1935)二本松孝蔵「名和神社拝殿」（『東洋建築』1巻4号、相模書房、1937） * [[Inaba Shrine]] buildings (1932–1940)『伊奈波神社略誌』伊奈波神社、1941年 * [[Minatogawa Shrine]] gate (1943)神山極三「湊川神社の神門」（『旅と伝説』16巻7号、1943年） == Publications == * "Japanese Architectural Techniques" series (Research on Hinoki Bark Roofing, Thatched Roofing, and Wooden Shingle Roofing), 1937–38 * "The Tower," {{ill|Dainikkō|ja|大日光}}, 1957 * "Repair of Shrines and Temples at Nikko," in {{ill|Ancient Architecture|ja|古建築}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] * {{ill|Jingiin Construction Division|ja|神祇院造営課}} * [[Kogakuin University]] * [[Sumiyoshi Ryō]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:20th-century Japanese architects]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Home Ministry officials]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kogakuin University alumni]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:People from Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:1884 births]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:1972 deaths]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:二本松孝蔵]]

In 1958, Nihonmatsu was awarded the Tochigi Prefecture Cultural Merit Award, and in 1966, he received the Fourth Class Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Works == === Designs === [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka District (1913)速水清孝・狩野勝重「建築技師・二本松孝蔵の経歴と「天野家住宅」について」（『日本建築学会東北支部研究報告集. 計画系』2016-06） * [[Yamauchi Shrine]] buildings (1932; destroyed during air raids)『別格官幣社山内神社御造営誌』山内豊信公・山内豊範公別格官幣社奉祀期成会、1935。 * [[Izumo Taisha]] envoy hall, ritual hall, and office (1933)二本松孝蔵「出雲大社勅使館齋館社務所の建築」（『東洋建築』1巻2号、相模書房、1937） * [[Saitama Gokoku Shrine]] buildings (1934)『埼玉県史』第七巻、埼玉県、1939年 * [[Nawa Shrine]] buildings (1935)二本松孝蔵「名和神社拝殿」（『東洋建築』1巻4号、相模書房、1937） * [[Inaba Shrine]] buildings (1932–1940)『伊奈波神社略誌』伊奈波神社、1941年 * [[Minatogawa Shrine]] gate (1943)神山極三「湊川神社の神門」（『旅と伝説』16巻7号、1943年） == Publications == * "Japanese Architectural Techniques" series (Research on Hinoki Bark Roofing, Thatched Roofing, and Wooden Shingle Roofing), 1937–38 * "The Tower," {{ill|Dainikkō|ja|大日光}}, 1957 * "Repair of Shrines and Temples at Nikko," in {{ill|Ancient Architecture|ja|古建築}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] * {{ill|Jingiin Construction Division|ja|神祇院造営課}} * [[Kogakuin University]] * [[Sumiyoshi Ryō]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:20th-century Japanese architects]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Home Ministry officials]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Kogakuin University alumni]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:People from Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:1884 births]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:1972 deaths]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[ja:二本松孝蔵]]

others | significant_projects = | significant_design = | literary_works = | awards = }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, March 26, 1884『大衆人事録 昭和15年改訂13版』570頁。 – September 9, 1972) was a Japanese [[architect]] and cultural property repair technician. He was involved in numerous preservation and repair projects and designed many new shrine and temple buildings. == Career == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. He graduated from Kote School (now [[Kogakuin University]]) in 1908. In 1911, he became the chief for repairs to [[National Treasures of Japan|national treasure]] buildings, including the repair of the Jizō Hall at [[Enmeiji]]. In 1917, he became a technical officer at the Imperial Shrine and Meiji Shrine. In 1927, he established the Kokui Architecture Research Institute『建設界紳士録 関東編』1962年、769頁。. His work included projects such as the construction of commemorative buildings for the Great Taisho Earthquake and designs for shrines and temples in coordination with the {{ill|Jingiin Construction Division|ja|神祇院造営課}}, including various prefectural shrines and [[Gokoku shrines]]『宗教公論』第27巻10号、宗教問題研究所1957年、10頁。. After the war, Nihonmatsu considered starting a business making umbrellas in his hometown「時の人＝二本松孝蔵」（『毎日新聞』1960.10.29 東京朝刊） but resumed his architectural career. In 1946, he was appointed a repair technician for [[Mount Hiei]]'s [[Enryakuji]]. From 1948, he worked on large-scale preservation efforts at [[Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] as a technician in its maintenance department. His office was renamed the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute『日本叙勲者名鑑 昭和39年4月〜昭和53年4月 上』、1978年、1414頁。. In 1958, Nihonmatsu was awarded the Tochigi Prefecture Cultural Merit Award, and in 1966, he received the Fourth Class Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Works == === Designs === [[File:名和神社 - 1937–38 * "The Tower," {{ill|Dainikkō|ja|大日光}}, 1957 * "Repair of Shrines and Temples at Nikko," in {{ill|Ancient Architecture|ja|古建築}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[Nikkō {{Infobox architect | image = File:二本松孝蔵.png | image_size = 200px | caption = From ''Record of the Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine'' | name = Nihonmatsu Kōzō
二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = {{Birth date|1884|3|26}} | birth_place = {{ill|Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|ja|小高町 (福島県相馬郡)}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1972|9|9|1884|3|26}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}} | practice_name = {{ill|Home Ministry (Japan)|ja|内務省}}
{{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}}
{{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} | significant_buildings= {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)|ja|伊奈波神社}}
Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}}. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Design === * {{ill|Nawa Shrine|ja|名和神社}} (1935) * {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)|ja|伊奈波神社}} (1932–1940)''Inaba Shrine Digest,'' 1941. * {{ill|Minatogawa Shrine|ja|湊川神社}} Gate (1943)Kamiya Kyokuzō, "Minatogawa Shrine's Gate," ''Tabi to Densetsu,'' 1943. * {{ill|Oarai Isosaki Shrine|ja|大洗磯前神社}} First Torii (1963) === Publications === * "Architectural Techniques of Japan" (1937–1938), serialized in ''Tōyō Kenchiku''. * "Restoration of Nikkō Temples" (1957), published in ''Dainikkō''. == References == == Further Reading == * "Nihonmatsu Kōzō and Cultural Merit Award," ''Shūkyō Kōron,'' 1957. * Shinohara Naomichi and 二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = {{Birth date|1884|3|26}} | birth_place = {{ill|Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|ja|小高町 (福島県相馬郡)}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1972|9|9|1884|3|26}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}} | practice_name = {{ill|Home Ministry (Japan)|ja|内務省}}
{{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}}
{{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} | significant_buildings= {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)|ja|伊奈波神社}}
Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}}. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Design === * {{ill|Nawa Shrine|ja|名和神社}} (1935) * {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)|ja|伊奈波神社}} (1932–1940)''Inaba Shrine Digest,'' 1941. * {{ill|Minatogawa Shrine|ja|湊川神社}} Gate (1943)Kamiya Kyokuzō, "Minatogawa Shrine's Gate," ''Tabi to Densetsu,'' 1943. * {{ill|Oarai Isosaki Shrine|ja|大洗磯前神社}} First Torii (1963) === Publications === * "Architectural Techniques of Japan" (1937–1938), serialized in ''Tōyō Kenchiku''. * "Restoration of Nikkō Temples" (1957), published in ''Dainikkō''. == References == == Further Reading == * "Nihonmatsu Kōzō and Cultural Merit Award," ''Shūkyō Kōron,'' 1957. * Shinohara Naomichi and {{Infobox architect | image = File:二本松孝蔵.png | image_size = 200px | caption = From ''Record of the Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine'' | name = Nihonmatsu Kōzō
二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = {{Birth date|1884|3|26}} | birth_place = {{ill|Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|ja|小高町 (福島県相馬郡)}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1972|9|9|1884|3|26}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}} | practice_name = {{ill|Home Ministry (Japan)|ja|内務省}}
{{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}}
{{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} | significant_buildings= {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)|ja|伊奈波神社}}
Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}}. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:Nawa Shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka Ward ({{ill|1913|ja|1913年}})Hayami Kiyotaka, Kano Katsushige, "The Career of Architect Kōzō Nihonmatsu and 'Amano Family Residence,'" Japanese Architectural Institute Tohoku Branch Research Report Series, Planning, June 2016. * {{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1932|ja|1932年}}) ※Destroyed in an air raid."Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine Dedicated to Yamauchi Yūdo and Yamauchi Toyonori," Yamauchi Shrine Kiseikai, 1935. * {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine|ja|鹿児島神宮}} main shrine (unknown year) * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} envoy hall, Saikan (purification hall), and administrative office ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}).Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Construction of Izumo Taisha Envoy Hall, Saikan, and Administrative Office," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 2, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Odo Shrine|ja|小戸神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1934|ja|1934年}})Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Renovation Plans for Odo Shrine," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 1, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Saitama Gokoku Shrine|ja|埼玉県護国神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1934|ja|1934年}})*Saitama Prefecture History*, vol. 7, Saitama Prefecture, 1939. * {{ill|Nawa Shrine|ja|名和神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}})Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Main Shrine of Nawa Shrine," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 4, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Hirakiki Shrine|ja|枚聞神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1936|ja|1936年}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島県護国神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1937|ja|1937年}})Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Fukushima Prefecture Official Ritual Shinhoyama Shrine for the Honoring of Spirits," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 1, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1937|ja|1937年}})*History of Fukushima City*, vol. 5, Fukushima City Board of Education, 1975, p. 790. * {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎神宮}} Museum of Antiquities ({{ill|1940|ja|1940年}}) ※No longer exists.*Record of the 2600th Anniversary Commemorative Ceremony*, vol. 7, 1940. * {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1940|ja|1940年}}) * {{ill|Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine|ja|大宮氷川神社}} administrative office and envoy Saikan ({{ill|1940|ja|1940年}})Shinohara Naomichi, Watanabe Yōko, "Research on Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine: Sunami Takashi and the Kokui Architecture Research Institute," *Architectural History and Design*, 2016, pp. 359-360, Japanese Architectural Institute, 2016. * {{ill|Akita Gokoku Shrine|ja|秋田県護国神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1940|ja|1940年}}) ※No longer exists. * {{ill|Toyotamahime Shrine (Minamikyūshū)|ja|豊玉姫神社 (南九州市)}} main shrine ({{ill|1940|ja|1940年}})*Chiran Local History*, Chiran Town, 1962. * {{ill|Inaba Shrine|ja|伊奈波神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1932|ja|1932年}}-{{ill|1940|ja|1940年}})*Summary of Inaba Shrine*, Inaba Shrine, 1941. * {{ill|Miyagi Gokoku Shrine|ja|宮城県護国神社}} ({{ill|1942|ja|1942年}}) ※Destroyed in an air raid.*History of Miyagi Prefecture*, vol. 13 (Art and Architecture), Miyagi Prefecture History Publication Committee, 1980. * {{ill|Minatogawa Shrine|ja|湊川神社}} Gate ({{ill|1943|ja|1943年}})Kiyozō Kamiyama, "Gate of Minatogawa Shrine," *Travel and Tradition*, vol. 16, issue 7, 1943. * {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1948|ja|1948年}}) * {{ill|Sōjiji|ja|總持寺}} Great Ancestral Hall (with Tanaka Naitō) ({{ill|1962|ja|1962年}}) * {{ill|Ōarai Isosaki Shrine|ja|大洗磯前神社}} First Torii Gate (with Tanaka Naitō) ({{ill|1963|ja|1963年}})Research Records: Memorandum on Investigation into Relics of Former Shugendo Activities in Fukushima Prefecture (3) (http://www.anno-spero.org/renaissance/dentou/history/fukushima_3.html). === Publications === * "Architectural Techniques of Japan" (1937–1938), serialized in ''Tōyō Kenchiku''. * "Restoration of Nikkō Temples" (1957), published in ''Dainikkō''. == References == == Further Reading == * "Nihonmatsu Kōzō and Cultural Merit Award," ''Shūkyō Kōron,'' 1957. * Shinohara Naomichi and Watanabe Yōko, "Research on Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine," ''Architectural History and Design,'' 2016. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} * {{ill|Shrine Construction Division, Jingi-in|ja|神祇院造営課}} * {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}} * {{ill|Sunami Takashi|ja|角南隆}} * {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:20th-century Japanese architects]] [[Category:Officials of the Home Ministry of Japan]] [[Category:Kogakuin University alumni]] [[Category:People from Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:1884 births]] {{Infobox architect | image = File:二本松孝蔵.png | image_size = 200px | caption = From ''Record of the Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine'' | name = Nihonmatsu Kōzō
二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = {{Birth date|1884|3|26}} | birth_place = {{ill|Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|ja|小高町 (福島県相馬郡)}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1972|9|9|1884|3|26}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}} | practice_name = {{ill|Home Ministry (Japan)|ja|内務省}}
{{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}}
{{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} | significant_buildings= {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)|ja|伊奈波神社}}
Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}}. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:Nawa Shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka Ward ({{ill|1913|ja|1913年}})Hayami Kiyotaka, Kano Katsushige, "The Career of Architect Kōzō Nihonmatsu and 'Amano Family Residence,'" Japanese Architectural Institute Tohoku Branch Research Report Series, Planning, June 2016. * {{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1932|ja|1932年}}) ※Destroyed in an air raid."Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine Dedicated to Yamauchi Yūdo and Yamauchi Toyonori," Yamauchi Shrine Kiseikai, 1935. * {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine|ja|鹿児島神宮}} main shrine (unknown year) * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} envoy hall, Saikan (purification hall), and administrative office ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}).Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Construction of Izumo Taisha Envoy Hall, Saikan, and Administrative Office," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 2, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Odo Shrine|ja|小戸神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1934|ja|1934年}})Kōzō issue 4, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Hirakiki Shrine|ja|枚聞神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1936|ja|1936年}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島県護国神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1937|ja|1937年}})Kōzō Nihonmatsu, Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}}. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:Nawa Shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] {{Infobox architect | image = File:二本松孝蔵.png | image_size = 200px | caption = From ''Record of the Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine'' | name = Nihonmatsu Kōzō
二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = {{Birth date|1884|3|26}} | birth_place = {{ill|Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|ja|小高町 (福島県相馬郡)}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1972|9|9|1884|3|26}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}} | practice_name = {{ill|Home Ministry (Japan)|ja|内務省}}
{{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}}
{{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} | significant_buildings= {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)|ja|伊奈波神社}}
Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}}. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:Nawa Shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka Ward ({{ill|1913|ja|1913年}})Hayami Kiyotaka, Kano Katsushige, "The Career of Architect Kōzō Nihonmatsu and 'Amano Family Residence,'" Japanese Architectural Institute Tohoku Branch Research Report Series, Planning, June 2016. * {{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1932|ja|1932年}}) ※Destroyed in an air raid."Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine Dedicated to Yamauchi Yūdo and Yamauchi Toyonori," Yamauchi Shrine Kiseikai, 1935. * {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine|ja|鹿児島神宮}} main shrine (unknown year) * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} envoy hall, Saikan (purification hall), and administrative office ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}).Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Construction of Izumo Taisha Envoy Hall, Saikan, and Administrative Office," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 2, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Odo Shrine|ja|小戸神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1934|ja|1934年}})Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Renovation Plans for Odo Shrine," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 1, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Saitama Gokoku Shrine|ja|埼玉県護国神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1934|ja|1934年}})*Saitama Prefecture History*, vol. 7, Saitama Prefecture, 1939. * {{ill|Nawa Shrine|ja|名和神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}})Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Main Shrine of Nawa Shrine," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 4, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Hirakiki Shrine|ja|枚聞神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1936|ja|1936年}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島県護国神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1937|ja|1937年}})Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Fukushima Prefecture Official Ritual Shinhoyama Shrine for the Honoring of Spirits," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 1, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社}} main {{Infobox architect | image = File:二本松孝蔵.png | image_size = 200px | caption = From ''Record of the Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine'' | name = Nihonmatsu Kōzō
二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = {{Birth date|1884|3|26}} | birth_place = {{ill|Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|ja|小高町 (福島県相馬郡)}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1972|9|9|1884|3|26}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}} | practice_name = {{ill|Home Ministry (Japan)|ja|内務省}}
{{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}}
{{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} | significant_buildings= {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)|ja|伊奈波神社}}
Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}}. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:Nawa Shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka Ward ({{ill|1913|ja|1913年}})Hayami Kiyotaka, Kano Katsushige, "The Career of Architect Kōzō Nihonmatsu and 'Amano Family Residence,'" Japanese Architectural Institute Tohoku Branch Research Report Series, Planning, June 2016. * {{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1932|ja|1932年}}) ※Destroyed in an air raid."Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine Dedicated to Yamauchi Yūdo and Yamauchi Toyonori," Yamauchi Shrine Kiseikai, 1935. * {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine|ja|鹿児島神宮}} main shrine (unknown year) * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} envoy hall, Saikan (purification hall), and administrative office ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}).Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Construction of Izumo Taisha Envoy Hall, Saikan, and Administrative Office," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 2, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Odo Shrine|ja|小戸神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1934|ja|1934年}})Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Renovation Plans for Odo Shrine," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 1, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Saitama Gokoku Shrine|ja|埼玉県護国神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1934|ja|1934年}})*Saitama Prefecture History*, vol. 7, Saitama Prefecture, 1939. * {{ill|Nawa Shrine|ja|名和神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}})Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Main Shrine of Nawa Shrine," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 4, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Hirakiki Shrine|ja|枚聞神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1936|ja|1936年}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島県護国神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1937|ja|1937年}})Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Fukushima Prefecture Official Ritual Shinhoyama Shrine for the Honoring of Spirits," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 1, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1937|ja|1937年}})*History of Fukushima City*, vol. 5, Fukushima City Board of Education, 1975, p. 790. * {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎神宮}} Museum of Antiquities ({{ill|1940|ja|1940年}}) ※No longer exists.*Record of the 2600th Anniversary Commemorative Ceremony*, vol. 7, 1940. * {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1940|ja|1940年}}) * {{ill|Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine|ja|大宮氷川神社}} administrative office and envoy Saikan ({{ill|1940|ja|1940年}})Shinohara Naomichi, Watanabe Yōko, "Research on Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine: Sunami Takashi and the Kokui Architecture Research Institute," *Architectural History and Design*, 2016, pp. 359-360, Japanese Architectural Institute, 2016. * {{ill|Akita Gokoku Shrine|ja|秋田県護国神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1940|ja|1940年}}) ※No longer exists. * {{ill|Toyotamahime Shrine (Minamikyūshū)|ja|豊玉姫神社 (南九州市)}} main shrine ({{ill|1940|ja|1940年}})*Chiran Local History*, Chiran Town, 1962. * {{ill|Inaba Shrine|ja|伊奈波神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1932|ja|1932年}}-{{ill|1940|ja|1940年}})*Summary of Inaba Shrine*, Inaba Shrine, 1941. * {{ill|Miyagi Gokoku Shrine|ja|宮城県護国神社}} ({{ill|1942|ja|1942年}}) ※Destroyed in an air raid.*History of Miyagi Prefecture*, vol. 13 (Art and Architecture), Miyagi Prefecture History Publication Committee, 1980. * {{ill|Minatogawa Shrine|ja|湊川神社}} Gate ({{ill|1943|ja|1943年}})Kiyozō Kamiyama, "Gate of Minatogawa Shrine," *Travel and Tradition*, vol. 16, issue 7, 1943. * {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1948|ja|1948年}}) * {{ill|Sōjiji|ja|總持寺}} Great Ancestral Hall (with Tanaka Naitō) ({{ill|1962|ja|1962年}}) * {{ill|Ōarai Isosaki Shrine|ja|大洗磯前神社}} First Torii Gate (with Tanaka Naitō) ({{ill|1963|ja|1963年}})Research Records: Memorandum on Investigation into Relics of Former Shugendo Activities in Fukushima Prefecture (3) (http://www.anno-spero.org/renaissance/dentou/history/fukushima_3.html). === Publications === * "Architectural Techniques of Japan" (1937–1938), serialized in ''Tōyō Kenchiku''. * "Restoration of Nikkō Temples" (1957), published in ''Dainikkō''. == References == == Further Reading == * "Nihonmatsu Kōzō and Cultural Merit Award," ''Shūkyō Kōron,'' 1957. * Shinohara Naomichi and Watanabe Yōko, "Research on Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine," ''Architectural History and Design,'' 2016. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} * By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:Nawa Shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}}. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:Nawa In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}}. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:Nawa Shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}}. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:Nawa Shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}}. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:Nawa Shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] {{Infobox architect | image = File:二本松孝蔵.png | image_size = 200px | caption = From ''Record of the Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine'' | name = Nihonmatsu Kōzō
二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = {{Birth date|1884|3|26}} | birth_place = {{ill|Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|ja|小高町 (福島県相馬郡)}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1972|9|9|1884|3|26}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}} | practice_name = {{ill|Home Ministry (Japan)|ja|内務省}}
{{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}}
{{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} | significant_buildings= {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)|ja|伊奈波神社}}
Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}}. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:Nawa Shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}}. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. Minatogawa Shrine," *Travel and Tradition*, vol. 16, issue 7, 1943. * {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1948|ja|1948年}}) * {{ill|Sōjiji|ja|總持寺}} Great Ancestral Hall (with Tanaka Naitō) ({{ill|1962|ja|1962年}}) * {{ill|Ōarai Isosaki Shrine|ja|大洗磯前神社}} First Torii Gate (with Tanaka Naitō) ({{ill|1963|ja|1963年}})Research Records: Memorandum on Investigation into Relics of Former Shugendo Activities in Fukushima Prefecture (3) (http://www.anno-spero.org/renaissance/dentou/history/fukushima_3.html). === Publications === * "Architectural Techniques of Japan" (1937–1938), {{Infobox architect | image = File:二本松孝蔵.png | image_size = 200px | caption = From ''Record of the Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine'' | name = Nihonmatsu Kōzō
二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = {{Birth date|1884|3|26}} | birth_place = {{ill|Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|ja|小高町 (福島県相馬郡)}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1972|9|9|1884|3|26}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}} | practice_name = {{ill|Home Ministry (Japan)|ja|内務省}}
{{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}}
{{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} | significant_buildings= {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)|ja|伊奈波神社}}
Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}}. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:Nawa Shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka Ward ({{ill|1913|ja|1913年}})Hayami Kiyotaka, Kano Katsushige, "The Career of Architect Kōzō Nihonmatsu and 'Amano Family Residence,'" Japanese Architectural Institute Tohoku Branch Research Report Series, Planning, June 2016. * {{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1932|ja|1932年}}) ※Destroyed in an air raid."Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine Dedicated to Yamauchi Yūdo and Yamauchi Toyonori," Yamauchi Shrine Kiseikai, 1935. * {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine|ja|鹿児島神宮}} main shrine (unknown year) * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} envoy hall, Saikan (purification hall), and administrative office ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}).Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Construction of Izumo Taisha Envoy Hall, Saikan, and Administrative Office," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 2, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Odo Shrine|ja|小戸神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1934|ja|1934年}})Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Renovation Plans for Odo Shrine," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 1, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Saitama Gokoku Shrine|ja|埼玉県護国神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1934|ja|1934年}})*Saitama Prefecture History*, vol. 7, Saitama Prefecture, 1939. * {{ill|Nawa Shrine|ja|名和神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}})Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Main Shrine of Nawa Shrine," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 4, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Hirakiki Shrine|ja|枚聞神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1936|ja|1936年}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島県護国神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1937|ja|1937年}})Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Fukushima Prefecture Official Ritual Shinhoyama Shrine for the Honoring of Spirits," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 1, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1937|ja|1937年}})*History of Fukushima City*, vol. 5, Fukushima City Board of Education, 1975, p. 790. * {{ill|Miyazaki Shrine|ja|宮崎神宮}} Museum of Antiquities ({{ill|1940|ja|1940年}}) ※No longer exists.*Record of the 2600th Anniversary Commemorative Ceremony*, vol. 7, 1940. * {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1940|ja|1940年}}) * {{ill|Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine|ja|大宮氷川神社}} administrative office and envoy Saikan ({{ill|1940|ja|1940年}})Shinohara Naomichi, Watanabe Yōko, "Research on Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine: Sunami Takashi and the Kokui Architecture Research Institute," *Architectural History and Design*, 2016, pp. 359-360, Japanese Architectural Institute, 2016. * {{ill|Akita Gokoku Shrine|ja|秋田県護国神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1940|ja|1940年}}) ※No longer exists. * {{ill|Toyotamahime Shrine (Minamikyūshū)|ja|豊玉姫神社 (南九州市)}} main shrine ({{ill|1940|ja|1940年}})*Chiran Local History*, Chiran Town, 1962. * {{ill|Inaba Shrine|ja|伊奈波神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1932|ja|1932年}}-{{ill|1940|ja|1940年}})*Summary of Inaba Shrine*, Inaba Shrine, 1941. * {{ill|Miyagi Gokoku Shrine|ja|宮城県護国神社}} ({{ill|1942|ja|1942年}}) ※Destroyed in an air raid.*History of Miyagi Prefecture*, vol. 13 (Art and Architecture), Miyagi Prefecture History Publication Committee, 1980. * {{ill|Minatogawa Shrine|ja|湊川神社}} Gate ({{ill|1943|ja|1943年}})Kiyozō Kamiyama, "Gate of Minatogawa Shrine," *Travel and Tradition*, vol. 16, issue 7, 1943. * {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1948|ja|1948年}}) * {{ill|Sōjiji|ja|總持寺}} Great Ancestral Hall (with Tanaka Naitō) ({{ill|1962|ja|1962年}}) * {{ill|Ōarai Isosaki Shrine|ja|大洗磯前神社}} First Torii Gate (with Tanaka Naitō) ({{ill|1963|ja|1963年}})Research Records: Memorandum on Investigation into Relics of Former Shugendo Activities in Fukushima Prefecture (3) (http://www.anno-spero.org/renaissance/dentou/history/fukushima_3.html). === Publications === * "Architectural Techniques of Japan" (1937–1938), serialized in ''Tōyō Kenchiku''. * "Restoration of Nikkō Temples" (1957), published in ''Dainikkō''. == References == == Further Reading == * "Nihonmatsu Kōzō and Cultural Merit Award," ''Shūkyō Kōron,'' 1957. * Shinohara Naomichi and Watanabe Yōko, "Research on Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine," ''Architectural History and Design,'' 2016. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} * {{ill|Shrine Construction Division, Jingi-in|ja|神祇院造営課}} * {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}} * {{ill|Sunami Takashi|ja|角南隆}} * {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:20th-century 二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = {{Birth date|1884|3|26}} | birth_place = {{ill|Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|ja|小高町 (福島県相馬郡)}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1972|9|9|1884|3|26}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}} | practice_name = {{ill|Home Ministry (Japan)|ja|内務省}}

Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}}. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:Nawa Shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka Ward ({{ill|1913|ja|1913年}})Hayami Kiyotaka, Kano Katsushige, "The Career of Architect Kōzō Nihonmatsu and 'Amano Family Residence,'" Japanese Architectural Institute Tohoku Branch Research Report Series, Planning, June 2016. * {{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1932|ja|1932年}}) ※Destroyed in an air raid."Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine Dedicated to Yamauchi Yūdo and Yamauchi Toyonori," Yamauchi Shrine Kiseikai, 1935. * {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine|ja|鹿児島神宮}} main shrine (unknown year) * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} envoy hall, Saikan (purification hall), and administrative office ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}).Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Construction of Izumo Taisha Envoy Hall, Saikan, and Administrative Office," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 2, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Odo Shrine|ja|小戸神社}} main shrine ({{ill|1934|ja|1934年}})Kōzō Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}}. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:Nawa Shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]]
