revision.comment: 11 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:二本松孝蔵 up to rev 100157249
revision.text:
{{Infobox 建築家 |image = File:二本松孝蔵.png |image_size = 200px |caption = 『別格官幣社山内神社御造営誌』より |name = にほんまつ こうぞう
二本松 孝蔵 |nationality = {{JPN}} |birth_date = [[1884年]][[3月26日]] |birth_place = [[福島県]]相馬郡小高町岡田 |death_date = {{死亡年月日と没年齢|1884|3|26|1972|9|9}} |death_place = 東京都 |alma_mater = 工手学校（現[[工学院大学]]） |practice_name = [[内務省 (日本)|内務省]]
国粋建築研究所
[[日光東照宮]] |significant_buildings= [[伊奈波神社]]
など |significant_projects = |significant_design = |literary_works = |awards = }} '''二本松 孝蔵'''（にほんまつ こうぞう、[[1884年]]（[[明治]]17年）[[3月26日]]『大衆人事録 昭和15年改訂13版』570頁。 - [[1972年]]（[[昭和]]47年）[[9月9日]]）は、日本の[[建築家]]、[[文化財]]修理技師。多数の社寺の保存修理や新築[[設計]]を手掛けた。 == 経歴等 == 福島県出身。[[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）工手学校（現[[工学院大学]]）卒業。[[1911年]]（[[明治]]44年）、[[国宝]]建造物修理工事主任。[[延命寺]]地蔵堂修理などを手掛ける。[[1917年]]（[[大正]]6年）に造神宮技手兼[[明治神宮]]造営技手となったのち、[[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）国粋建築研究所を開設『建設界紳士録 関東編』1962年、769頁。。[[1925年]]（[[大正]]14年）大正大震災記念建造物競技設計佳作。[[角南隆]]を中心とした[[神祇院造営課]]と連動し、[[神社]]では11府県社の[[護国神社]]や[[官国幣社]]の社務所、齋館などの設計のほか、[[寺院]]でも[[比叡山]]修理、[[身延山]]祖廟設営など社寺ともに多くの設計を手掛けた『宗教公論』第27巻10号、宗教問題研究所1957年、10頁。。 戦後は一時故郷でカラカサを作ろうかとも考えたというが「時の人＝二本松孝蔵」（『毎日新聞』1960.10.29 東京朝刊）、[[1946年]]（[[昭和]]21年）[[比叡山延暦寺]]修理技師拝命につづき、[[1948年]]（[[昭和]]23年）から[[日光東照宮]][[営繕]]課技師を拝命し、日光国宝保存工事事務所技師として昭和の大修理に尽力した。事務所は社寺建築研究所と改めている『日本叙勲者名鑑 昭和39年4月〜昭和53年4月 上』、1978年、1414頁。 [[1958年]]（昭和33年）、[[栃木県]]文化功労章、[[1966年]]（昭和41年）4月勲四等[[瑞宝章]]。 == 作品 == ===設計=== [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|名和神社]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|豊玉姫神社]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|伊奈波神社]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|湊川神社神門]] * 小高区天野家住宅（[[1913年]]）速水清孝・狩野勝重「建築技師・二本松孝蔵の経歴と「天野家住宅」について」（『日本建築学会東北支部研究報告集. 計画系』2016-06） * [[山内神社]]社殿（[[1932年]]）※空襲焼失。『別格官幣社山内神社御造営誌』山内豊信公・山内豊範公別格官幣社奉祀期成会、1935。 * [[鹿児島神宮]]社殿（[[?年]]） * [[出雲大社]]勅使館、齋館、社務所（[[1933年]]）。二本松孝蔵「出雲大社勅使館齋館社務所の建築」（『東洋建築』1巻2号、相模書房、1937） * [[小戸神社]]社殿（[[1934年]]）二本松孝蔵「小戶神社の改築計畫」（『東洋建築』1巻1号、相模書房、1937）。 * [[埼玉県護国神社]]社殿（[[1934年]]）『埼玉県史』第七巻、埼玉県、1939年 * [[名和神社]]社殿（[[1935年]]）二本松孝蔵「名和神社拝殿」（『東洋建築』1巻4号、相模書房、1937） * [[枚聞神社]]社殿（[[1936年]]） * [[福島県護国神社]]社殿（[[1937年]]）二本松孝蔵「福島縣官祭信夫山招魂社」（『東洋建築』1巻1号、相模書房、1937） * [[福島稲荷神社]]社殿（[[1937年]]）『福島市史』第5巻、福島市教育委員会、1975年、790頁 * [[宮崎神宮]]徴古館（[[1940年]]）※現存せず『紀元二千六百年祝典記録』第七冊、1940年 * [[栃木県護国神社]]社殿（[[1940年]]） * [[大宮氷川神社]]社務所、勅使齋館（[[1940年]]）篠原直通・渡辺 洋子「大宮氷川神社に関する研究 角南隆と国粋建築研究所」（『建築歴史・意匠』 (2016) 359-360 日本建築学会、2016年 ） * [[秋田県護国神社]]社殿（[[1940年]]）※現存せず * [[豊玉姫神社 (南九州市)]]社殿（[[1940年]]）『知覧郷土史』知覧町、1962年 * [[伊奈波神社]]社殿（[[1932年]]-[[1940年]]）『伊奈波神社略誌』伊奈波神社、1941年 * [[宮城県護国神社]]（[[1942年]]）※空襲焼失『宮城県史』13（美術・建築）宮城県史刊行会、1980年 * [[湊川神社]]神門（[[1943年]]）神山極三「湊川神社の神門」（『旅と伝説』16巻7号、1943年） * [[鹿児島県護国神社]]社殿（[[1948年]]） * [[總持寺]]大祖堂（内藤多仲共）（[[1962年]]） * [[大洗磯前神社]]一の鳥居（内藤多仲共）（[[1963年]]）調査実績:福島県内に残る旧修験者活動遺構調査に関する覚書（3）（http://www.anno-spero.org/renaissance/dentou/history/fukushima_3.html） == 著作等 == * 「日本建築技法講座」（1）〜（3）（檜皮葺の研究、萱葺の研究、柿葺の硏究） （『東洋建築』1巻8号-2巻1号、相模書房、1937-38年） * 「塔」（『大日光』9号、日光東照宮、1957年） * 「日光社寺殿堂修理に就而」（『古建築』） == 出典 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 「二本松孝蔵翁文化功労章を受く」（『宗教公論』第27巻10号、宗教問題研究所、1957年、10頁）。 * 篠原直通・渡辺 洋子「大宮氷川神社に関する研究 角南隆と国粋建築研究所」（『建築歴史・意匠』 (2016) 359-360 日本建築学会、2016年 == 関連項目 == * [[日光東照宮]] * [[神祇院造営課]] * [[工学院大学]] * [[角南隆]] * [[護国神社]] {{Normdaten}} {{デフォルトソート:にほんまつ こうぞう}} [[Category:20世紀日本の建築家]] [[Category:内務省 (日本)|築]] [[Category:工学院大学出身の人物]] [[Category:福島県出身の人物]] [[Category:1884年生]] [[Category:1972年没]]

など |significant_projects = |significant_design = |literary_works = |awards = }} '''二本松 孝蔵'''（にほんまつ こうぞう、[[1884年]]（[[明治]]17年）[[3月26日]]『大衆人事録 昭和15年改訂13版』570頁。 - [[1972年]]（[[昭和]]47年）[[9月9日]]）は、日本の[[建築家]]、[[文化財]]修理技師。多数の社寺の保存修理や新築[[設計]]を手掛けた。 == 経歴等 == 福島県出身。[[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）工手学校（現[[工学院大学]]）卒業。[[1911年]]（[[明治]]44年）、[[国宝]]建造物修理工事主任。[[延命寺]]地蔵堂修理などを手掛ける。[[1917年]]（[[大正]]6年）に造神宮技手兼[[明治神宮]]造営技手となったのち、[[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）国粋建築研究所を開設『建設界紳士録 関東編』1962年、769頁。。[[1925年]]（[[大正]]14年）大正大震災記念建造物競技設計佳作。[[角南隆]]を中心とした[[神祇院造営課]]と連動し、[[神社]]では11府県社の[[護国神社]]や[[官国幣社]]の社務所、齋館などの設計のほか、[[寺院]]でも[[比叡山]]修理、[[身延山]]祖廟設営など社寺ともに多くの設計を手掛けた『宗教公論』第27巻10号、宗教問題研究所1957年、10頁。。 戦後は一時故郷でカラカサを作ろうかとも考えたというが「時の人＝二本松孝蔵」（『毎日新聞』1960.10.29 東京朝刊）、[[1946年]]（[[昭和]]21年）[[比叡山延暦寺]]修理技師拝命につづき、[[1948年]]（[[昭和]]23年）から[[日光東照宮]][[営繕]]課技師を拝命し、日光国宝保存工事事務所技師として昭和の大修理に尽力した。事務所は社寺建築研究所と改めている『日本叙勲者名鑑 昭和39年4月〜昭和53年4月 上』、1978年、1414頁。 [[1958年]]（昭和33年）、[[栃木県]]文化功労章、[[1966年]]（昭和41年）4月勲四等[[瑞宝章]]。 == 作品 == ===設計=== [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|名和神社]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|豊玉姫神社]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|伊奈波神社]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|湊川神社神門]] * 小高区天野家住宅（[[1913年]]）速水清孝・狩野勝重「建築技師・二本松孝蔵の経歴と「天野家住宅」について」（『日本建築学会東北支部研究報告集. 計画系』2016-06） * [[山内神社]]社殿（[[1932年]]）※空襲焼失。『別格官幣社山内神社御造営誌』山内豊信公・山内豊範公別格官幣社奉祀期成会、1935。 * [[鹿児島神宮]]社殿（[[?年]]） * [[出雲大社]]勅使館、齋館、社務所（[[1933年]]）。二本松孝蔵「出雲大社勅使館齋館社務所の建築」（『東洋建築』1巻2号、相模書房、1937） * [[小戸神社]]社殿（[[1934年]]）二本松孝蔵「小戶神社の改築計畫」（『東洋建築』1巻1号、相模書房、1937）。 * [[埼玉県護国神社]]社殿（[[1934年]]）『埼玉県史』第七巻、埼玉県、1939年 * [[名和神社]]社殿（[[1935年]]）二本松孝蔵「名和神社拝殿」（『東洋建築』1巻4号、相模書房、1937） * [[枚聞神社]]社殿（[[1936年]]） * [[福島県護国神社]]社殿（[[1937年]]）二本松孝蔵「福島縣官祭信夫山招魂社」（『東洋建築』1巻1号、相模書房、1937） * [[福島稲荷神社]]社殿（[[1937年]]）『福島市史』第5巻、福島市教育委員会、1975年、790頁 * [[宮崎神宮]]徴古館（[[1940年]]）※現存せず『紀元二千六百年祝典記録』第七冊、1940年 * [[栃木県護国神社]]社殿（[[1940年]]） * [[大宮氷川神社]]社務所、勅使齋館（[[1940年]]）篠原直通・渡辺 洋子「大宮氷川神社に関する研究 角南隆と国粋建築研究所」（『建築歴史・意匠』 (2016) 359-360 日本建築学会、2016年 ） * [[秋田県護国神社]]社殿（[[1940年]]）※現存せず * [[豊玉姫神社 (南九州市)]]社殿（[[1940年]]）『知覧郷土史』知覧町、1962年 * [[伊奈波神社]]社殿（[[1932年]]-[[1940年]]）『伊奈波神社略誌』伊奈波神社、1941年 * [[宮城県護国神社]]（[[1942年]]）※空襲焼失『宮城県史』13（美術・建築）宮城県史刊行会、1980年 * [[湊川神社]]神門（[[1943年]]）神山極三「湊川神社の神門」（『旅と伝説』16巻7号、1943年） * [[鹿児島県護国神社]]社殿（[[1948年]]） * [[總持寺]]大祖堂（内藤多仲共）（[[1962年]]） * [[大洗磯前神社]]一の鳥居（内藤多仲共）（[[1963年]]）調査実績:福島県内に残る旧修験者活動遺構調査に関する覚書（3）（http://www.anno-spero.org/renaissance/dentou/history/fukushima_3.html） == 著作等 == * 「日本建築技法講座」（1）〜（3）（檜皮葺の研究、萱葺の研究、柿葺の硏究） （『東洋建築』1巻8号-2巻1号、相模書房、1937-38年） * 「塔」（『大日光』9号、日光東照宮、1957年） * 「日光社寺殿堂修理に就而」（『古建築』） == 出典 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 「二本松孝蔵翁文化功労章を受く」（『宗教公論』第27巻10号、宗教問題研究所、1957年、10頁）。 * 篠原直通・渡辺 洋子「大宮氷川神社に関する研究 角南隆と国粋建築研究所」（『建築歴史・意匠』 (2016) 359-360 日本建築学会、2016年 == 関連項目 == * [[日光東照宮]] * [[神祇院造営課]] * [[工学院大学]] * [[角南隆]] * [[護国神社]] {{Normdaten}} {{デフォルトソート:にほんまつ こうぞう}} [[Category:20世紀日本の建築家]] [[Category:内務省 (日本)|築]] [[Category:工学院大学出身の人物]] [[Category:福島県出身の人物]] [[Category:1884年生]] [[Category:1972年没]]

二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = {{Birth date|1884|3|26}} | birth_place = {{ill|Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|ja|小高町 (福島県相馬郡)}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1972|9|9|1884|3|26}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}} | practice_name = {{ill|Home Ministry (Japan)|ja|内務省}}

Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学}}. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:Nawa Shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] {{Infobox architect | image = File:二本松孝蔵.png | image_size = 200px | caption = From ''Record of the Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine'' | name = Nihonmatsu Kōzō
二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = {{Birth date|1884|3|26}} | birth_place = {{ill|Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|ja|小高町 (福島県相馬郡)|12=draft}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1972|9|9|1884|3|26}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学|12=draft}} | practice_name = {{ill|Home Ministry (Japan)|ja|内務省|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|12=draft}}
{{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|12=draft}} | significant_buildings= {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)|ja|伊奈波神社|12=draft}}
Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学|12=draft}}. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺|12=draft}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|12=draft}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山|12=draft}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|12=draft}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:Nawa Shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] {{Infobox architect | image = File:二本松孝蔵.png | image_size = 200px | caption = From ''Record of the Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine'' | name = Nihonmatsu Kōzō
二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = {{Birth date|1884|3|26}} | birth_place = {{ill|Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|ja|小高町 (福島県相馬郡)|qq=draft}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1972|9|9|1884|3|26}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学|qq=draft}} | practice_name = {{ill|Home Ministry (Japan)|ja|内務省|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft}} | significant_buildings= {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)|ja|伊奈波神社|qq=draft}}
Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学|qq=draft}}. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺|qq=draft}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山|qq=draft}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200x200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka Ward ({{ill|1913|ja|1913rd year|qq=draft}})Hayami Kiyotaka, Kano Katsushige, "The Career of Architect Kōzō Nihonmatsu and 'Amano Family Residence,'" Japanese Architectural Institute Tohoku Branch Research Report Series, Planning, June 2016. * {{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社|qq=draft}} main shrine ({{ill|1932|ja|1932nd year|qq=draft}}) ※Destroyed in an air raid."Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine Dedicated to Yamauchi Yūdo and Yamauchi Toyonori," Yamauchi Shrine Kiseikai, 1935. * {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine|ja|鹿児島神宮|qq=draft}} main shrine (unknown year) * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|qq=draft}} envoy hall, Saikan (purification hall), and administrative office ({{ill|1933|ja|1933rd year|qq=draft}}).Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Construction of Izumo Taisha Envoy Hall, Saikan, and Administrative Office," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 2, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Odo {{Infobox architect | image = File:二本松孝蔵.png | image_size = 200px | caption = From ''Record of the Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine'' | name = Nihonmatsu Kōzō
二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = {{Birth date|1884|3|26}} | birth_place = {{ill|Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|ja|小高町 (福島県相馬郡)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|qq=}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1972|9|9|1884|3|26}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogakuin University|qq=}} | practice_name = {{ill|Home Ministry (Japan)|ja|内務省|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Home Ministry (Japan)|qq=}}
{{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=}}
{{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Tōshō-gū|qq=}} | significant_buildings= {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)|ja|伊奈波神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Shrine (Gifu)|qq=}}
Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogakuin University|qq=}}. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji|qq=}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hiei|qq=}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Tōshō-gū|qq=}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200x200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka Ward ({{ill|1913|ja|1913rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1913|qq=}})Hayami Kiyotaka, Kano Katsushige, "The Career of Architect Kōzō Nihonmatsu and 'Amano Family Residence,'" Japanese Architectural Institute Tohoku Branch Research Report Series, Planning, June 2016. * {{ill|Yamauchi {{Infobox architect | image = File:二本松孝蔵.png | image_size = 200px | caption = From ''Record of the Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine'' | name = Nihonmatsu Kōzō
二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = {{Birth date|1884|3|26}} | birth_place = {{ill|Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|ja|小高町 (福島県相馬郡)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|qq=}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1972|9|9|1884|3|26}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogakuin University|qq=}} | practice_name = {{ill|Home Ministry (Japan)|ja|内務省|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Home Ministry (Japan)|qq=}}
{{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=}}
{{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Tōshō-gū|qq=}} | significant_buildings= {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)|ja|伊奈波神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Shrine (Gifu)|qq=}}
Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogakuin University|qq=}}. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji|qq=}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hiei|qq=}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Tōshō-gū|qq=}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200x200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka Ward ({{ill|1913|ja|1913rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1913|qq=}})Hayami Kiyotaka, Kano Katsushige, "The Career of Architect Kōzō Nihonmatsu and 'Amano Family Residence,'" Japanese Architectural Institute Tohoku Branch Research Report Series, Planning, June 2016. * {{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ({{ill|1932|ja|1932nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1932|qq=}}) ※Destroyed in an air {{Infobox architect | image = File:二本松孝蔵.png | image_size = 200px | caption = From ''Record of the Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine'' | name = Nihonmatsu Kōzō
二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = {{Birth date|1884|3|26}} | birth_place = {{ill|Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|ja|小高町 (福島県相馬郡)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|qq=}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1972|9|9|1884|3|26}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogakuin University|qq=}} | practice_name = {{ill|Home Ministry (Japan)|ja|内務省|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Home Ministry (Japan)|qq=}}
{{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=}}
{{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Tōshō-gū|qq=}} | significant_buildings= {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)|ja|伊奈波神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Shrine (Gifu)|qq=}}
Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogakuin University|qq=}}. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji|qq=}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hiei|qq=}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Tōshō-gū|qq=}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200x200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka Ward ({{ill|1913|ja|1913rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1913|qq=}})Hayami Kiyotaka, Kano Katsushige, "The Career of Architect Kōzō Nihonmatsu and 'Amano Family Residence,'" Japanese Architectural Institute Tohoku Branch Research Report Series, Planning, June 2016. * {{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ({{ill|1932|ja|1932nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1932|qq=}}) ※Destroyed in an air 二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = {{Birth date|1884|3|26}} | birth_place = {{ill|Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|ja|小高町 (福島県相馬郡)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|qq=}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1972|9|9|1884|3|26}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogakuin University|qq=}} | practice_name = {{ill|Home Ministry (Japan)|ja|内務省|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Home Ministry (Japan)|qq=}}
{{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=}}
{{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Tōshō-gū|qq=}} | significant_buildings= {{ill|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)|ja|伊奈波神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Shrine (Gifu)|qq=}}
Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogakuin University|qq=}}. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji|qq=}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hiei|qq=}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Tōshō-gū|qq=}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200x200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka Ward ({{ill|1913|ja|1913rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1913|qq=}})Hayami Kiyotaka, Kano Katsushige, "The Career of Architect Kōzō Nihonmatsu and 'Amano Family Residence,'" Japanese Architectural Institute Tohoku Branch Research Report Series, Planning, June 2016. * {{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ({{ill|1932|ja|1932nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1932|qq=}}) ※Destroyed in an air raid."Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine Dedicated to Yamauchi Yūdo and Yamauchi Toyonori," Yamauchi Shrine Kiseikai, 1935. * {{ill|Kagoshima Jingu|lt=Kagoshima Jingu|lt=Kagoshima His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hiei|qq=}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now {{ill|Kogakuin University|ja|工学院大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogakuin University|qq=}}. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji|qq=}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hiei|qq=}}''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Tōshō-gū|qq=}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200x200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Tōshō-gū|qq=}} during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:名和神社 - 二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = {{Birth date|1884|3|26}} | birth_place = {{ill|Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|ja|小高町 (福島県相馬郡)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|qq=}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1972|9|9|1884|3|26}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = [[:en:Kogakuin University|Kogakuin University]] | practice_name = [[:en:Interior ministry|Home Ministry (Japan)]]

[[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] | significant_buildings= [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)]]
Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now [[:en:Kogakuin University|Kogakuin University]]. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji|qq=}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at [[:en:Mount Hiei|Mount Hiei]]''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200x200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka Ward ([[:en:1913|1913]])Hayami Kiyotaka, Kano Katsushige, "The Career of Architect Kōzō Nihonmatsu and 'Amano Family Residence,'" Japanese Architectural Institute Tohoku Branch Research Report Series, Planning, June 2016. * {{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1932|1932]]) ※Destroyed in an air raid."Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine Dedicated to Yamauchi Yūdo and Yamauchi Toyonori," Yamauchi Shrine Kiseikai, 1935. * [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Shrine]] main shrine (unknown year) * [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] envoy hall, Saikan (purification hall), and administrative office ([[:en:1933|1933]]).Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Construction of Izumo Taisha Envoy Hall, Saikan, and Administrative Office," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 2, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Odo Shrine|ja|小戸神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odo Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1934|1934]])Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Renovation Plans for Odo Shrine," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 1, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Saitama Gokoku Shrine|ja|埼玉県護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1934|1934]])*Saitama Prefecture History*, vol. 7, Saitama Prefecture, 1939. * [[:en:Nawa Shrine|Nawa Shrine]] main shrine ([[:en:1935|1935]])Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Main Shrine of Nawa Shrine," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 4, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * [[:en:Hirasaki Shrine|Hirakiki Shrine]] main shrine ([[:en:1936|1936]]) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島県護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1937|1937]])Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Fukushima Prefecture Official Ritual Shinhoyama Shrine for the Honoring of Spirits," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 1, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Inari Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1937|1937]])*History of Fukushima City*, vol. 5, Fukushima City Board of Education, 1975, p. 790. * [[:en:Miyazaki Jingū|Miyazaki Shrine]] Museum of Antiquities ([[:en:1940|1940]]) ※No longer exists.*Record of the 2600th Anniversary Commemorative Ceremony*, vol. 7, 1940. * {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1940|1940]]) * {{ill|Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine|ja|大宮氷川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine|qq=}} administrative office and envoy Saikan ([[:en:1940|1940]])Shinohara Naomichi, Watanabe Yōko, "Research on Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine: Sunami Takashi and the Kokui Architecture Research Institute," *Architectural History and Design*, 2016, pp. 359-360, Japanese Architectural Institute, 2016. * {{ill|Akita Gokoku Shrine|ja|秋田県護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1940|1940]]) ※No longer exists. * {{ill|Toyotamahime Shrine (Minamikyūshū)|ja|豊玉姫神社 (南九州市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotamahime Shrine (Minamikyūshū)|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1940|1940]])*Chiran Local History*, Chiran Town, 1962. * [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]] main shrine ([[:en:1932|1932]]-[[:en:1940|1940]])*Summary of Inaba Shrine*, Inaba Shrine, 1941. * {{ill|Miyagi Gokoku Shrine|ja|宮城県護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:1942|1942]]) ※Destroyed in an air raid.*History of Miyagi Prefecture*, vol. 13 (Art and Architecture), Miyagi Prefecture History Publication Committee, 1980. * [[:en:Minatogawa Shrine|Minatogawa Shrine]] Gate ([[:en:1943|1943]])Kiyozō Kamiyama, "Gate of Minatogawa Shrine," *Travel and Tradition*, vol. 16, issue 7, 1943. * {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1948|1948]]) * [[:en:Sōji-ji|Sōjiji]] Great Ancestral Hall (with Tanaka Naitō) ([[:en:1962|1962]]) * [[:en:Oarai Isosaki Shrine|Ōarai Isosaki Shrine]] First Torii Gate (with Tanaka Naitō) ([[:en:1963|1963]])Research Records: Memorandum on Investigation into Relics of Former Shugendo Activities in Fukushima Prefecture (3) (http://www.anno-spero.org/renaissance/dentou/history/fukushima_3.html). === Publications === * "Architectural Techniques of Japan" (1937–1938), serialized in ''Tōyō Kenchiku''. * "Restoration of Nikkō Temples" (1957), published in ''Dainikkō''. == References == == Further Reading == * "Nihonmatsu Kōzō and Cultural Merit Award," ''Shūkyō revision.text:
{{Infobox architect | image = File:二本松孝蔵.png | image_size = 200px | caption = From ''Record of the Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine'' | name = Nihonmatsu Kōzō
二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = {{Birth date|1884|3|26}} | birth_place = {{ill|Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|ja|小高町 (福島県相馬郡)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|qq=}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1972|9|9|1884|3|26}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = [[:en:Kogakuin University|Kogakuin University]] | practice_name = [[:en:Interior ministry|Home Ministry (Japan)]]
{{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=}}
[[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] | significant_buildings= [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)]]
Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now [[:en:Kogakuin University|Kogakuin University]]. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji|qq=}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at [[:en:Mount Hiei|Mount Hiei]]''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200x200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka Ward ([[:en:1913|1913]])Hayami Kiyotaka, Kano Katsushige, "The Career of Architect Kōzō Nihonmatsu and 'Amano Family Residence,'" Japanese Architectural Institute Tohoku Branch Research Report Series, Planning, June 2016. * {{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1932|1932]]) ※Destroyed in an air raid."Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine Dedicated to Yamauchi Yūdo and Yamauchi Toyonori," Yamauchi Shrine Kiseikai, 1935. * [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Shrine]] main shrine (unknown year) * [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] envoy hall, Saikan (purification hall), and administrative office ([[:en:1933|1933]]).Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Construction of Izumo Taisha Envoy Hall, Saikan, and Administrative Office," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 2, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Odo Shrine|ja|小戸神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odo Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1934|1934]])Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Renovation Plans for Odo Shrine," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 1, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Saitama Gokoku Shrine|ja|埼玉県護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1934|1934]])*Saitama Prefecture History*, vol. 7, Saitama Prefecture, 1939. * [[:en:Nawa Shrine|Nawa Shrine]] main shrine ([[:en:1935|1935]])Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Main Shrine of Nawa Shrine," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 4, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * [[:en:Hirasaki Shrine|Hirakiki Shrine]] main shrine ([[:en:1936|1936]]) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島県護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1937|1937]])Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Fukushima Prefecture Official Ritual Shinhoyama Shrine for the Honoring of Spirits," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 1, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Inari Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1937|1937]])*History of Fukushima City*, vol. 5, Fukushima City Board of {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1940|1940]]) * {{ill|Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine: Sunami Takashi and the Kokui Architecture Research Institute," *Architectural History and Design*, 2016, pp. 359-360, Japanese Architectural Institute, 2016. * {{ill|Akita Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1948|1948]]) * [[:en:Sōji-ji|Sōjiji]] Great Ancestral Hall (with Tanaka Naitō) ([[:en:1962|1962]]) * [[:en:Oarai Isosaki Shrine|Ōarai Isosaki Shrine]] First Torii Gate (with Tanaka Naitō) ([[:en:1963|1963]])Research Records: Memorandum on Investigation into Relics of Former Shugendo Activities in Fukushima Prefecture (3) (http://www.anno-spero.org/renaissance/dentou/history/fukushima_3.html). === Publications === * "Architectural Techniques of Japan" (1937–1938), 二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = {{Birth date|1884|3|26}} | birth_place = {{ill|Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|ja|小高町 (福島県相馬郡)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|qq=}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1972|9|9|1884|3|26}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = [[:en:Kogakuin University|Kogakuin University]] | practice_name = [[:en:Interior ministry|Home Ministry (Japan)]]
{{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=}}
[[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] | significant_buildings= [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)]]
Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now [[:en:Kogakuin University|Kogakuin University]]. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji|qq=}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at [[:en:Mount Hiei|Mount Hiei]]''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200x200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka Ward ([[:en:1913|1913]])Hayami Kiyotaka, Kano Katsushige, "The Career of Architect Kōzō Nihonmatsu and 'Amano Family Residence,'" Japanese Architectural Institute Tohoku Branch Research Report Series, Planning, June 2016. * {{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1932|1932]]) ※Destroyed in an air raid."Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine Dedicated to Yamauchi Yūdo and Yamauchi Toyonori," Yamauchi Shrine Kiseikai, 1935. * [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Shrine]] main shrine (unknown year) * [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] envoy hall, Saikan (purification hall), and administrative office ([[:en:1933|1933]]).Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Construction of Izumo Taisha Envoy Hall, Saikan, and Administrative Office," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 2, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Odo Shrine|ja|小戸神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odo Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1934|1934]])Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Renovation Plans for Odo Shrine," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 1, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Saitama Gokoku Shrine|ja|埼玉県護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1934|1934]])*Saitama Prefecture History*, vol. 7, Saitama Prefecture, 1939. * [[:en:Nawa Shrine|Nawa Shrine]] main shrine ([[:en:1935|1935]])Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Main Shrine of Nawa Shrine," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 4, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * [[:en:Hirasaki Shrine|Hirakiki Shrine]] main shrine ([[:en:1936|1936]]) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島県護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1937|1937]])Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Fukushima Prefecture Official Ritual Shinhoyama Shrine for the Honoring of Spirits," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 1, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * Education, 1975, p. 790. * [[:en:Miyazaki Jingū|Miyazaki Shrine]] Museum of Antiquities ([[:en:1940|1940]]) ※No longer exists.*Record of the 2600th Anniversary Commemorative Ceremony*, vol. 7, 1940. * Shrine|ja|大宮氷川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine|qq=}} administrative office and envoy Saikan ([[:en:1940|1940]])Shinohara Naomichi, Watanabe Yōko, "Research on Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine: Sunami Takashi and the Kokui Architecture Research Institute," *Architectural History and Design*, 2016, pp. 359-360, Japanese Architectural Institute, 2016. * {{ill|Akita Gokoku (南九州市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotamahime Shrine (Minamikyūshū)|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1940|1940]])*Chiran Local History*, Chiran Town, 1962. * [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]] main shrine ([[:en:1932|1932]]-[[:en:1940|1940]])*Summary of Inaba Shrine*, Inaba Shrine, 1941. * {{ill|Miyagi Gokoku Shrine|ja|宮城県護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:1942|1942]]) ※Destroyed in an air raid.*History of Miyagi Prefecture*, vol. 13 (Art and Architecture), Miyagi Prefecture History Publication Committee, 1980. * [[:en:Minatogawa Shrine|Minatogawa Shrine]] Gate ([[:en:1943|1943]])Kiyozō Kamiyama, "Gate of Minatogawa Shrine," *Travel and Tradition*, vol. 16, issue 7, 1943. * {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1948|1948]]) * [[:en:Sōji-ji|Sōjiji]] Great Ancestral Hall (with Tanaka Naitō) ([[:en:1962|1962]]) * [[:en:Oarai Isosaki Shrine|Ōarai Isosaki Shrine]] First Torii Gate (with Tanaka Naitō) ([[:en:1963|1963]])Research Records: Memorandum on Investigation into Relics of Former Shugendo Activities in Fukushima Prefecture (3) (http://www.anno-spero.org/renaissance/dentou/history/fukushima_3.html). === Publications === * "Architectural Techniques of Japan" (1937–1938), serialized in ''Tōyō Kenchiku''. * "Restoration of Nikkō Temples" (1957), published in ''Dainikkō''. == References == == Further Reading == * "Nihonmatsu Kōzō and Cultural Merit Award," ''Shūkyō Kōron,'' 1957. * Shinohara Naomichi and Watanabe Yōko, "Research on Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine," ''Architectural History and Design,'' 2016. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] * {{Infobox architect | image = File:二本松孝蔵.png | image_size = 200px | caption = From ''Record of the Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine'' | name = Nihonmatsu Kōzō
二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = {{Birth date|1884|3|26}} | birth_place = {{ill|Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|ja|小高町 (福島県相馬郡)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|qq=}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1972|9|9|1884|3|26}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = [[:en:Kogakuin University|Kogakuin University]] | practice_name = [[:en:Interior ministry|Home Ministry (Japan)]]
{{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=}}
[[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] | significant_buildings= [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)]]
Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now [[:en:Kogakuin University|Kogakuin University]]. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji|qq=}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at [[:en:Mount Hiei|Mount Hiei]]''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200x200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka Ward ([[:en:1913|1913]])Hayami Kiyotaka, Kano Katsushige, "The Career of Architect Kōzō Nihonmatsu and 'Amano Family Residence,'" Japanese Architectural Institute Tohoku Branch Research Report Series, Planning, June 2016. * {{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1932|1932]]) ※Destroyed in an air raid."Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine Dedicated to Yamauchi Yūdo and Yamauchi Toyonori," Yamauchi Shrine Kiseikai, 1935. * [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Shrine]] main shrine (unknown year) * [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] envoy hall, Saikan (purification hall), and administrative office ([[:en:1933|1933]]).Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Construction of Izumo Taisha Envoy Hall, Saikan, and Administrative Office," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 2, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Odo Shrine|ja|小戸神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odo Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1934|1934]])Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Renovation Plans for Odo Shrine," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. vol. 7, Saitama Prefecture, 1939. * [[:en:Nawa Shrine|Nawa Shrine]] main shrine ([[:en:1935|1935]])Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Main Shrine of Nawa Shrine," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 4, Sagami Shobō, {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
[[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] | significant_buildings= [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)]]
Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[:en:1884|1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[1908]], he graduated from what is now [[:en:Kogakuin University|Kogakuin University]]. By [[1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji|qq=}}. In [[1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at [[:en:Mount Hiei|Mount Hiei]]''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200x200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka Ward ([[:en:1913|1913]])Hayami Kiyotaka, Kano Katsushige, "The Career of Architect Kōzō Nihonmatsu and 'Amano Family Residence,'" Japanese Architectural Institute Tohoku Branch Research Report Series, Planning, June 2016. * {{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1932|1932]]) ※Destroyed in an air raid."Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine Dedicated to Yamauchi Yūdo and Yamauchi Toyonori," Yamauchi Shrine Kiseikai, 1935. * [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Shrine]] main shrine (unknown year) * [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] envoy hall, Saikan (purification hall), and administrative office ([[:en:1933|1933]]).Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Construction of Izumo Taisha Envoy Hall, Saikan, and Administrative Office," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 2, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Odo Shrine|ja|小戸神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odo Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1934|1934]])Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Renovation Plans for Odo Shrine," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 1, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Saitama Gokoku Shrine|ja|埼玉県護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Gokoku Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 埼玉縣護國神社}} main shrine ([[:en:1934|1934]])*Saitama Prefecture History*, vol. 7, Saitama Prefecture, 1939. * [[:en:Nawa Shrine|Nawa Shrine]] main shrine ([[:en:1935|1935]])Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Main Shrine of Nawa Shrine," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 4, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * [[:en:Hirasaki Shrine|Hirakiki Shrine]] main shrine ([[:en:1936|1936]]) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku {{Infobox architect | image = File:二本松孝蔵.png | image_size = 200px | caption = From ''Record of the Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine'' | name = Nihonmatsu Kōzō
二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = {{Birth date|1884|3|26}} | birth_place = {{ill|Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|ja|小高町 (福島県相馬郡)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1972|9|9|1884|3|26}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = [[:en:Kogakuin University|Kogakuin University]] | practice_name = [[:en:Interior ministry|Home Ministry (Japan)]]
{{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
[[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] | significant_buildings= [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)]]
Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[:en:1884|1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[:en:1908|1908]], he graduated from what is now [[:en:Kogakuin University|Kogakuin University]]. By [[:en:1911|1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji|qq=}}. In [[:en:1917|1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at [[:en:Mount Hiei|Mount Hiei]]''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200x200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka Ward ([[:en:1913|1913]])Hayami Kiyotaka, Kano Katsushige, "The Career of Architect Kōzō Nihonmatsu and 'Amano Family Residence,'" Japanese Architectural Institute Tohoku Branch Research Report Series, Planning, June 2016. * {{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1932|1932]]) ※Destroyed in an air raid."Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine Dedicated to Yamauchi Yūdo and Yamauchi Toyonori," Yamauchi Shrine Kiseikai, 1935. * [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Shrine]] main shrine (unknown year) * [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] envoy hall, Saikan (purification hall), and administrative office ([[:en:1933|1933]]).Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Construction of Izumo Taisha Envoy Hall, Saikan, and Administrative Office," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 2, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Odo Shrine|ja|小戸神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odo Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1934|1934]])Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Renovation Plans for Odo Shrine," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 1, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Saitama Gokoku Shrine|ja|埼玉県護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Gokoku Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 埼玉縣護國神社}} main shrine ([[:en:1934|1934]])*Saitama Prefecture History*, vol. 7, Saitama Prefecture, 1939. * [[:en:Nawa Shrine|Nawa Shrine]] main shrine ([[:en:1935|1935]])Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Main Shrine of Nawa Shrine," {{Infobox architect | image = File:二本松孝蔵.png | image_size = 200px | caption = From ''Record of the Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine'' | name = Nihonmatsu Kōzō
二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = {{Birth date|1884|3|26}} | birth_place = {{ill|Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|ja|小高町 (福島県相馬郡)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1972|9|9|1884|3|26}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = [[:en:Kogakuin University|Kogakuin University]] | practice_name = [[:en:Interior ministry|Home Ministry (Japan)]]
{{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
[[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] | significant_buildings= [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)]]
Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[:en:1884|1884]] [[March 26]] – [[1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[:en:1908|1908]], he graduated from what is now [[:en:Kogakuin University|Kogakuin University]]. By [[:en:1911|1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji|qq=}}. In [[:en:1917|1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1927|1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at [[:en:Mount Hiei|Mount Hiei]]''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[:en:1958|1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200x200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka Ward ([[:en:1913|1913]])Hayami Kiyotaka, Kano Katsushige, "The Career of Architect Kōzō Nihonmatsu and 'Amano Family Residence,'" Japanese Architectural Institute Tohoku Branch Research Report Series, Planning, June 2016. * {{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1932|1932]]) ※Destroyed in an air raid."Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine Dedicated to Yamauchi Yūdo and Yamauchi Toyonori," Yamauchi Shrine Kiseikai, 1935. * [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Shrine]] main shrine (unknown year) * [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] envoy hall, Saikan (purification hall), and administrative office ([[:en:1933|1933]]).Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Construction of Izumo Taisha Envoy Hall, Saikan, and Administrative Office," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 2, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Odo Shrine|ja|小戸神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odo Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1934|1934]])Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Renovation Plans for Odo Shrine," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 1, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Saitama Gokoku Shrine|ja|埼玉県護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Gokoku Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 埼玉縣護國神社}} main shrine ([[:en:1934|1934]])*Saitama Prefecture History*, vol. 7, Saitama Prefecture, 1939. * [[:en:Nawa Shrine|Nawa Shrine]] main shrine ([[:en:1935|1935]])Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Main Shrine of Nawa Shrine," {{Infobox architect | image = File:二本松孝蔵.png | image_size = 200px | caption = From ''Record of the Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine'' | name = Nihonmatsu Kōzō
二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = {{Birth date|1884|3|26}} | birth_place = {{ill|Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|ja|小高町 (福島県相馬郡)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1972|9|9|1884|3|26}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = [[:en:Kogakuin University|Kogakuin University]] | practice_name = [[:en:Interior ministry|Home Ministry (Japan)]]
{{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
[[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] | significant_buildings= [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)]]
Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[:en:1884|1884]] [[March 26]] – [[:en:1972|1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[:en:1908|1908]], he graduated from what is now [[:en:Kogakuin University|Kogakuin University]]. By [[:en:1911|1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji|qq=}}. In [[:en:1917|1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1927|1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at [[:en:Mount Hiei|Mount Hiei]]''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[:en:1958|1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[:en:1966|1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200x200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka Ward ([[:en:1913|1913]])Hayami Kiyotaka, Kano Katsushige, "The Career of Architect Kōzō {{Infobox architect | image = File:二本松孝蔵.png | image_size = 200px | caption = From ''Record of the Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine'' | name = Nihonmatsu Kōzō
二本松 孝蔵 | nationality = {{JPN}} | birth_date = {{Birth date|1884|3|26}} | birth_place = {{ill|Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|ja|小高町 (福島県相馬郡)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odaka Town, Soma District, Fukushima|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1972|9|9|1884|3|26}} | death_place = Tokyo | alma_mater = [[:en:Kogakuin University|Kogakuin University]] | practice_name = [[:en:Interior ministry|Home Ministry (Japan)]]
{{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
[[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] | significant_buildings= [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine (Gifu)]]
Others | awards = Order of the Sacred Treasure, 4th Class }} '''Nihonmatsu Kōzō''' (二本松 孝蔵, [[:en:1884|1884]] [[March 26]] – [[:en:1972|1972]] [[September 9]]) was a Japanese architect and conservation specialist for cultural properties. He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[:en:1908|1908]], he graduated from what is now [[:en:Kogakuin University|Kogakuin University]]. By [[:en:1911|1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[National Treasures of Japan]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji|qq=}}. In [[:en:1917|1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1927|1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at [[:en:Mount Hiei|Mount Hiei]]''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[:en:1958|1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[:en:1966|1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200x200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka Ward ([[:en:1913|1913]])Hayami Kiyotaka, Kano Katsushige, "The Career of Architect Kōzō Nihonmatsu and 'Amano Family Residence,'" Japanese Architectural Institute Tohoku Branch Research Report Series, Planning, June 2016. * {{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1932|1932]]) ※Destroyed in an air raid."Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine Dedicated to Yamauchi Yūdo and Yamauchi Toyonori," Yamauchi Shrine Kiseikai, 1935. * [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Shrine]] main shrine (unknown year) * [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] envoy hall, Saikan (purification hall), and administrative office ([[:en:1933|1933]]).Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Construction of Izumo Taisha Envoy Hall, Saikan, and Administrative Office," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 2, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Odo Shrine|ja|小戸神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odo Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1934|1934]])Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Renovation Plans for Odo Shrine," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 1, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Saitama Gokoku Shrine|ja|埼玉県護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Gokoku Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 埼玉縣護國神社}} main shrine ([[:en:1934|1934]])*Saitama Prefecture History*, vol. 7, Saitama Prefecture, 1939. * [[:en:Nawa Shrine|Nawa Shrine]] main shrine ([[:en:1935|1935]])Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Main Shrine of Nawa Shrine," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 4, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * [[:en:Hirasaki Shrine|Hirakiki Shrine]] main shrine ([[:en:1936|1936]]) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku He is renowned for his preservation, restoration, and design of numerous shrines and temples. == Biography == Nihonmatsu was born in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]''Taishū Jinjiroku'', 1940 Edition, p. 570.. In [[:en:1908|1908]], he graduated from what is now [[:en:Kogakuin University|Kogakuin University]]. By [[:en:1911|1911]], he was supervising the repair of [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure (Japan)]] such as the restoration of Jizō Hall at {{ill|Enmei-ji|ja|延命寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji|qq=}}. In [[:en:1917|1917]], he became a technical officer for shrine construction, later founding the {{ill|Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|ja|国粋建築研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusui Architecture Research Institute|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1927|1927]]''Kensetsukai Shinsuroku Kantō-hen,'' 1962, p. 769.. His designs for shrines and temples include projects like the [[Minobu-san]] Mausoleum and extensive repair work at [[:en:Mount Hiei|Mount Hiei]]''Shūkyō Kōron,'' Vol. 27, No. 10, 1957, p. 10.. After [[World War II]], Nihonmatsu continued as a conservation expert, leading major restoration efforts for [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]] during the Shōwa period. He renamed his office the Shrine and Temple Architecture Research Institute''Nihon Jōkunsya Meikan,'' 1978, p. 1414.. In [[:en:1958|1958]], he received the Cultural Merit Award from [[Tochigi Prefecture]], and in [[:en:1966|1966]] he was awarded the Fourth Order of the Sacred Treasure. == Notable Works == === Designs === [[File:名和神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200x200px|Nawa Shrine]] [[File:Toyotamahime Shrine honden.JPG|thumb|200px|Toyotamahime Shrine]] [[File:Inaba-jinja Gifu Japan06-rjpg.jpg|thumb|200px|Inaba Shrine]] [[File:Minatogawa Shrine Gate.jpg|thumb|200px|Minatogawa Shrine Gate]] * Amano Family Residence, Odaka Ward ([[:en:1913|1913]])Hayami Kiyotaka, Kano Katsushige, "The Career of Architect Kōzō Nihonmatsu and 'Amano Family Residence,'" Japanese Architectural Institute Tohoku Branch Research Report Series, Planning, June 2016. * {{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1932|1932]]) ※Destroyed in an air raid."Reconstruction of Yamauchi Shrine Dedicated to Yamauchi Yūdo and Yamauchi Toyonori," Yamauchi Shrine Kiseikai, 1935. * [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Shrine]] main shrine (unknown year) * [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] envoy hall, Saikan (purification hall), and administrative office ([[:en:1933|1933]]).Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Construction of Izumo Taisha Envoy Hall, Saikan, and Administrative Office," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 2, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Odo Shrine|ja|小戸神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odo Shrine|qq=}} main shrine ([[:en:1934|1934]])Kōzō Nihonmatsu, "Renovation Plans for Odo Shrine," *Eastern Architecture*, vol. 1, issue 1, Sagami Shobō, 1937. * {{ill|Saitama Gokoku Shrine|ja|埼玉県護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Gokoku Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 埼玉縣護國神社}} main shrine
